target.title: perf(agents): memoize per-message token pressure on precheck path
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` (the per-turn pre-prompt token-pressure precheck on the embedded-runner path) reduces over the entire transcript on every prompt submission via `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` → `estimateMessageTokenPressure`. The per-message work is recomputed every turn even when only a single message has been appended since the last call, so the wall-time cost of the precheck grows linearly with session length on the hot path before the provider request is sent.

Why does this matter now?

Tool-heavy long sessions pay multi-millisecond per-turn CPU on the precheck. Synthetic 1000-message tool-heavy transcripts measure ≈ 2.5 ms / turn pre-cache; with very large `toolCall.arguments` and tool-result text the per-turn cost reaches ≈ 100 ms at 4000 messages. None of this is dominant on its own, but it is purely pre-dispatch local CPU that the same turn pays over and over with no semantic change to the inputs.

What is the intended outcome?

Memoize `estimateMessageTokenPressure` by message object identity using a process-local `WeakMap<object, number>`. Messages on the embedded-runner precheck path are append-only and not mutated in place once added to `activeSession.messages`, so identity is a safe cache key. The reducer in `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` now calls a thin `memoizedMessageTokenPressure` wrapper that returns the cached value on a hit and computes-then-stores on a miss. No semantic change: the numerical output of `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` and the `route` / `overflowTokens` / `estimatedPromptTokens` fields of `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` are byte-identical for any given input.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Wiring `llmBoundaryTokenPressure` from a real-rendered-transport-bytes anchor (the canonical-fact-forward direction introduced in #85541). This PR caches the fallback path; it does not compete with that direction.
- `estimateMessagesTokens` in `compaction.ts` (compaction-event path) and the existing prune loop in `compaction.ts`.
- `estimateToolResultReductionPotential` in `tool-result-truncation.ts`, which is the un-cached residual cost still present in the precheck wall time.
- Anthropic-side payload / cache-control work.
- Media path work.

What does success look like?

The committed replay artifact (`scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`) demonstrates the post-patch cold-to-warm wall-time ratio on a 1000-message tool-heavy synthetic transcript (≈ 4× on the run captured in Evidence below). Earlier local profiling against an uncached build of the same code showed per-turn savings in the 1.9–2.0× range on representative tool-heavy long sessions and 5–7× on a pathological extreme synthetic shape; this PR does not claim TTFT, p95/p99, or any provider-side improvement. No semantic change, all existing tests pass, six new behavior tests pin the cache contract.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The cache key contract: identity is a safe key only because the embedded-runner path treats messages as append-only. The new test "memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate" documents that cached estimates are tied to message identity, so callers must preserve the append-only / no-in-place-mutation invariant or add explicit invalidation.
- WeakMap lifecycle: the cache is bounded by live message objects and cannot leak.
- Boundary semantics around assistant tool-call arguments, tool-result content, and image blocks are unchanged.

## Identity-cache invariant and reproducible benchmark

Reproducible benchmark artifact: `scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`. It directly calls the production `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` helper used by the embedded-runner at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:4125`, with the same parameter shape and an `activeSession.messages`-shaped transcript. It does not run the full provider/request loop. It captures cold/warm/append-one timings plus route stability across 10 append turns.

Caller inventory for `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` (these are audited existing callers, not additional files changed by this PR):

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:4125` — embedded-runner prompt-submission loop. Messages source: `activeSession.messages` (pi-coding-agent state). Append-only; replacement via `state.messages = limited` creates new identities.
- `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts:515` — `openclaw compact` CLI. Messages source: `getSessionBranchMessages(sessionManager)` (read from session JSONL). Each CLI invocation loads from disk → new objects, no cross-call identity reuse.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-result-context-guard.ts:419` — mid-turn tool-result truncation guard. Messages source: `contextMessages` from `transformContext`. When truncation is needed, `cloneMessagesForGuard` (`tool-result-context-guard.ts:132–135`) creates new top-level identities, then `enforceToolResultLimitInPlace` (`185–199`) mutates only those clones. When no truncation is needed, `contextMessages === sourceMessages` and no in-place mutation runs.

External callers via plugin-sdk: `src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts:219` re-exports `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` and the rendered-boundary helper for plugin authors. The plugin-sdk re-export is not runtime-frozen; external plugin consumers could mutate message objects in place. Safety there is by documented input contract: callers that repeat estimates must treat message objects as immutable for the cache lifetime, or pass fresh message identities after mutation. The in-tree exported callers audited above do not mutate cached identities before repeated estimates. The contract is named in the doc-comment block above the `messageTokenPressureCache` declaration in `preemptive-compaction.ts` and pinned by the per-message identity cache test #2.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

This PR does not close an issue. It addresses a structural cost identified in local profiling that overlaps in shape with reports such as #76702 (Windows + Feishu DM slow large-session latency) and #86231 (Codex harness prompt latency overhead). It deliberately does not claim to resolve either, because neither has been root-caused to the precheck path; the per-turn precheck saving alone is too small to explain those reports.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #85541 (the PR that reshaped this estimator into `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` and introduced `llmBoundaryTokenPressure` as a caller-supplied anchor).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: per-turn `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` CPU wall time on the embedded-runner precheck path scales O(N) with transcript size and recomputes the same per-message token pressure on every turn for unchanged messages.
- Real environment tested: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic, Node 22.22.2, branch `feature/openclaw-preemptive-precheck-cache` on `8da8bc4aadfc7f62af864b24718896b538c069e3`. Production function `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` is the call under measurement, via the same import path the embedded-runner uses.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --version` → v22.22.2
  - `PROBE_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) pnpm exec tsx scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`
- Evidence after fix:

Live console output from running the committed replay artifact against the production embedded-runner precheck function — a copied terminal capture, not a unit test summary:

```
$ PROBE_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) pnpm exec tsx scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts
openclaw preemptive-precheck-replay (embedded-runner path)
commit                      03a0e995d50713da6f458c265a29fa5198ecdb7e
messages_in                 1010
budget_tokens               200000
reserve                     16000

cold_precheck_ms            7.460
warm_precheck_ms_mean       1.759 (20 repeats over the same transcript)
append_step_ms_mean         1.408 (10 turns, one new user msg each)

per-append measurements:
  turn  1 ms=   1.341 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1555832
  turn  2 ms=   1.344 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1555911
  turn  3 ms=   1.331 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1555990
  turn  4 ms=   1.598 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556069
  turn  5 ms=   1.395 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556148
  turn  6 ms=   1.334 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556228
  turn  7 ms=   1.893 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556307
  turn  8 ms=   1.309 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556386
  turn  9 ms=   1.262 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556465
  turn 10 ms=   1.269 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556544

final_route                 compact_then_truncate
final_estimatedPromptTokens 1556544
final_overflowTokens        1372544
final_promptBudgetBefRes    184000
```

- Observed result after fix: cold first scan pays the full per-message reducer once; subsequent scans against the same activeSession.messages-shaped transcript avoid recomputing the cached per-message estimate and drop to roughly a quarter of the cold time on this hardware. The route, `estimatedPromptTokens`, `overflowTokens`, and `promptBudgetBeforeReserve` returned by the production function remain semantically consistent across the 20 warm scans and grow monotonically across the 10 append-one turns — confirming the cache is returning correct values for each fresh message identity, not stale estimates.
- What was not tested: no live provider/channel ingress (no Anthropic/OpenAI call); no captured real session JSONL replay (the transcript is synthetic but uses the production message shape and the production exported helper); no Windows or non-x86_64 hardware run; no claim of TTFT or provider-side improvement.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: synthetic transcript, single machine, single Node version. Absolute numbers are hardware-dependent; the cold-to-warm ratio (≈ 4× here) is the part that generalizes. The replay harness can be re-run by any reviewer on a normal worktree (no provider credentials required).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

Listed under "Exact steps or command run after this patch" above.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Six new behavior tests in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`, all inside a new `describe("per-message identity cache", …)` block:

1. Same boundary estimate on repeat scans of the same messages.
2. Memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate (documents the identity-key contract).
3. Re-counts when a brand-new message identity is appended.
4. Does not double-count when the same message identity appears twice.
5. Preserves precheck `route` / `overflowTokens` / `estimatedPromptTokens` decisions across repeat calls.
6. Counts assistant tool-call argument bytes identically on repeat scans (covers the heavy-`JSON.stringify` path).

What failed before this fix, if known?

No prior test failures. The change is a performance optimization, not a correctness fix.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` returns byte-identical decisions for any given input. The only observable difference is reduced wall time on the precheck path in long tool-heavy sessions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The identity-cache invariant for callers outside `activeSession.messages`. The plugin-sdk re-export at `agent-harness-runtime.ts:219` means external plugin authors can call `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` themselves; if any such caller mutates a message object in place, the cached estimate is stale.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Caller inventory: every in-tree caller of `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` was inspected (see "Real behavior proof and identity-cache invariant" above). `attempt.ts` uses append-only `activeSession.messages`; `cli-compaction.ts` reads from disk each call (fresh objects); `tool-result-context-guard.ts` only mutates clones produced by `cloneMessagesForGuard`. None of the in-tree callers mutate cached message objects.
- Documented contract: the doc-comment block above `messageTokenPressureCache` names the invariant and explicitly cites the test that pins it.
- Behavior test: `preemptive-compaction.test.ts` per-message identity cache test #2 mutates a message's content after the first scan and asserts that the second scan returns the unchanged estimate, so any caller that introduces in-place mutation will see its expected fresh value silently shadowed by the cached value — the test will continue to document the contract.
- Repo policy alignment: process-local metadata caches are explicitly permitted by the repo `AGENTS.md` ("Process-local metadata caches ok when lifecycle-owned and bounded/single-slot. Freshness exceptions need named owner + tests"); this cache is process-local, lifecycle-bounded by the WeakMap-to-message reference, single-slot per message, and covered by the named test contract.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper review 2026-05-25T22:11Z requested two rank-up moves: (a) a reproducible benchmark artifact that exercises `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` on the embedded-runner path, and (b) caller-inventory confirmation that exported callers outside `activeSession.messages` cannot mutate cached message objects before a repeated estimate. Both are addressed above. Pre-submission self-review also softened benchmark wording ("synthetic … long-session-shaped" rather than "representative"), clarified the cached-estimate phrasing to match the measured table, removed a non-diff line reference (`compaction.ts:535`), and reframed the cache-mutation test as documenting the identity-key invariant rather than asserting a regression-failure.

target.title: feat(events): add audience field for hidden runtime-context system events
target.body:
## Summary

Addresses part of #69492 (system-event-shape consumer-path leakage; under umbrella #69208 Track B).

Adds an optional `audience: 'internal' | 'user-facing'` field to `SystemEvent`, defaulting to `'user-facing'` (full backward-compat for every existing caller). Internal-audience events are wrapped at the consumer integration point in the existing `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` delimiters with the canonical header lines from `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`. The agent runtime sees the content as runtime context; user-facing surfaces strip it via the already-installed `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers (`sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`, `memory-host-sdk/host/session-files.ts`, `agents/internal-events.ts`, history readers, Control UI extract).

Mirrors the producer-side runtime-context delimiter pattern landed in:
- `e918e5f75c` (#71761) — agent runtime-context custom entry
- `6e985a421d` — webchat runtime-context

No new persistence shape, no consumer-side filter (avoids the audit-bypass concern flagged on #69217), no display-layer per-surface patch (avoids the rejection pattern from #69366).

## Why this scope is tight

This primitive is the **hidden runtime-context lane only**. It is not a delivery-routing primitive. Events with a positive user delivery contract (exec completion via `notifyOnExit`, cron payloads, heartbeat acks) are NOT migrated — those have heartbeat-driven explicit-relay paths (`buildExecEventPrompt` / `buildCronEventPrompt`) plus tactical producer-side skips like `bd60df3e53` (`fix: silence cron exec completion noise`). Marking them internal would suppress delivery on regular reply turns where the model is told to keep wrapped content private.

The user-deliverable noise question stays open; the maintainer's recent pattern is case-by-case (`bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`), and that lane should remain.

## Changes

- `src/infra/system-events.ts` — add `SystemEventAudience` type and optional `audience` field on `SystemEvent` + `SystemEventOptions`. Default `"user-facing"`. JSDoc explicitly documents the scope (hidden runtime-context, not delivery routing) and the operator-surface caveat (`peekSystemEvents`/`peekSystemEventEntries` keep showing internal events for debugging). `areSystemEventsEqual` honors the field so duplicate-suppression and `consumeSystemEventEntries` prefix-match keep working.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/session-system-events.ts` — partition the drain output into user-facing lines and internal lines. User-facing lines emit as plain `System: ...` (unchanged). Internal lines are wrapped in `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN/END` with the canonical header (matching `formatAgentInternalEventsForPrompt`). `escapeInternalRuntimeContextDelimiters` handles adversarial inner tokens.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts` — migrate `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` to `audience: "internal"`. This event is queued only after the isolated agent run has already delivered to the user via its own channel (gated on `delivered === true` and `shouldQueueCronAwareness`); the main session needs hidden context, not a fake-user `System (untrusted): ...` bubble.

## Explicitly NOT migrated

- `maybeNotifyOnExit` (exec completion) — `notifyOnExit` delivery contract.
- `executeMainSessionCronJob` (cron payload) — user-scheduled reminder content.
- Cron auto-disable warning, cron failure alert — user-relevant.
- Cron `wake()` API — exposes arbitrary user-supplied text via gateway `cron.wake`.
- Gateway presence (`server-methods/system.ts`), node events (`server-node-events.ts`).
- Task registry, session-maintenance-warning, plugin runtime — caller-dependent / operator-facing / third-party.

JSDoc on `SystemEventAudience` explicitly excludes these from the lane and points at the existing delivery paths.

## Follow-up plan (out of scope for this PR)

This PR is intentionally one-step. It introduces the primitive and migrates exactly one producer (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) so the wrap-on-drain integration point can be reviewed against a real call site. No additional audience-PR is pre-committed; future migrations should be evaluated case-by-case as concrete leakage reports surface.

**Candidates that may earn `audience: "internal"` later if a real need is demonstrated:** debug- or awareness-style producers that are queued *after* the user has already received delivery on a different channel (the same shape as `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` here). Each would need its own justification — no batch migration.

**Explicitly NOT in any audience-PR sequence** (these stay in the maintainer's case-by-case tactical-patch lane, e.g. `bd60df3e53`, `3f63ba8fd808`):
- exec completion (`maybeNotifyOnExit`)
- cron payload delivery (`executeMainSessionCronJob`)
- heartbeat acks
- any other producer with a positive user delivery contract

A larger abstraction was floated during peer review — a 3-state shape (`prompt-visible` | `hidden-context` | `dedicated-delivery`) for events that have both an internal-awareness *and* a user-delivery contract. **Not on the roadmap.** That redesign collides with the heartbeat-runner explicit-relay paths and the producer-side skips the maintainer has been adding directly; pursuing it would re-open the rejection patterns from #69366. Flagging here only so future readers know it was considered and deliberately not pursued.

If maintainer direction differs, happy to follow guidance on #69492 before landing further migrations.

## Real behavior proof

> Refreshed 2026-05-26 after the force-rebase onto current `upstream/main` (`d0dd8b8a41`) + decoupling from `forceSenderIsOwnerFalse` / `trusted` / `QueuedSystemEvent` / `resolveEventOwnerDowngrade` (all removed upstream in `02182d5a30`, *refactor: remove sender owner tool gating*, 2026-05-21). The 9-commit codex-iteration ladder was squashed to one clean commit (`d5fc9d9609`) atop the post-refactor base. Lab evidence below is from a fresh run against that exact commit; prod-runtime evidence (Mercury's deployed gateway) is currently from the pre-rebase 9-commit build (same observable behavior, just additional sender-owner code Mercury was carrying that is now gone), and will be regenerated on the next `mercury/local-patches` deploy.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** cron-awareness system events (`queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`, queued onto the main session **after** the isolated cron has already delivered the result on its own channel) were appearing in the main session as user-visible `System: cron run …` bubbles, even though their purpose is internal awareness for the agent rather than a user-deliverable message. The fix migrates that producer to `audience: "internal"` so the autoreply integration site wraps the event in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` … `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` and the existing `stripInternalRuntimeContext` consumers remove it from every user-facing surface.
- **Real environment tested:** local sandbox on macOS Sonoma (Darwin 23.6.0), Node 25.6.0, pnpm v11.2.2, against branch `feat/system-event-audience-internal-routing` HEAD `d5fc9d9609`. Mercury's production OpenClaw gateway daemon (LaunchDaemon, 11-account Matrix deployment) has been running an earlier 2-commit local-patch variant of this PR continuously and shows the wrap-delimiter primitive working in real transcripts (excerpts retained below); the rebased squashed commit shares the same observable behavior and will be re-attested on the next prod reload.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:** (1) `git fetch upstream && git checkout d5fc9d9609`; (2) `CI=true pnpm install` (no native rebuild required); (3) `pnpm tsgo:core:test` (type-check); (4) `pnpm vitest run src/infra/system-events.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue.collect.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts` (touched-test surfaces); (5) `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` (end-to-end production-code-path harness against the actual `enqueueSystemEvent` → `drainFormattedSystemEvents` → `stripInternalRuntimeContext` pipeline, no mocks).
- **Evidence after fix (fresh harness output against rebased HEAD):**
  - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — clean exit 0.
  - `pnpm vitest run …` — `Test Files: 4 passed (4); Tests: 171 passed (171); Duration ~38s` (53 in `system-events.test.ts` covering default audience, round-trip, equality, wrap shape, ordering, user-facing-only, internal-only, escape, end-to-end strip, exec-shape filter bypass, heartbeat-noise filter bypass; 118 across `delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` + `queue.collect.test.ts` + `heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts` covering producer migration, drain semantics, heartbeat-path queueing).
  - **`scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` against HEAD `d5fc9d9609`** — drives the actual production code paths on a throwaway sessionKey, no network / matrix / cron involvement. Live terminal output (run 2026-05-26T15:49:37Z):
    ```
    === (A) post-enqueue queue snapshot ===
    [
      {
        "audience": "internal",
        "contextKey": "cron:proof-pr72201",
        "text": "Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only."
      }
    ]

    === (B) rendered prompt body (model-visible) ===
    <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    System: [2026-05-26T15:49:37Z] Cron landed: daily cost report delivered to user. Hidden runtime context only.
    <<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>

    --- wrap checks ---
      BEGIN delimiter present: true
      END delimiter present  : true
      END after BEGIN        : true
      canonical header line  : true
      event body present     : true

    === (C) user-facing view (after stripInternalRuntimeContext) ===
    ""

    --- strip checks ---
      no BEGIN in user view  : true
      no event body leaked   : true

    === RESULT ===
    PASS
    ```
    All three claims verified end-to-end on the rebased HEAD: (A) the event is queued with `audience: "internal"` (operator-side `peekSystemEventEntries` exposes it for debugging); (B) the prompt body the model sees is wrapped in `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` … `<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` with the canonical header lines; (C) `stripInternalRuntimeContext` reduces the user-facing view to an empty string — nothing from the internal event leaks to the transcript / history reader / channel surface. The harness lives at `scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` (local artifact, not pushed in this PR — output above is the captured terminal log).
  - **Pre-rebase prod evidence (retained — wrap primitive in the running gateway):** the wrap delimiters are already a proven primitive in Mercury's prod daemon. Live transcript excerpt from `~/.openclaw/agents/devin/sessions/3998fa7e-…03d.jsonl` showing the `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` / `OpenClaw runtime context (internal):` block landing in the user-message context (auto-stripped from user-visible surfaces by `stripInternalRuntimeContext`):
    ```
    [Sun 2026-05-03 21:14 EDT] <<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>
    OpenClaw runtime context (internal):
    This context is runtime-generated, not user-authored. Keep internal details private.

    [Internal task completion event]
    source: subagent
    session_key: agent:devin:subagent:9d4f2234-…
    ```
  - **Pre-rebase prod evidence (retained — cron isolated runs on the patched daemon):** redacted runtime log excerpt from `~/.openclaw/cron/runs/4309f6e7-f09d-4011-93ff-21726e3aa76d.jsonl`:
    ```json
    {"action":"finished","status":"ok","summary":"Top hit: openclaw-docs/automation/cron-jobs … [mercury | claude-sonnet-4-6]","sessionKey":"agent:mercury:cron:4309f6e7-…","durationMs":27268,"model":"gpt-5-mini","provider":"openai"}
    ```
    These are the exact runs whose post-delivery `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent` calls produce the events this PR migrates to `audience: "internal"`. Cron + main session have been continuously stable on the pre-rebase Mercury build; reload onto the squashed-rebase build is pending and will be re-attested.
  - **Local codex peer review:** `codex review --base upstream/main` against the rebased HEAD reports *"no discrete, actionable regressions in the diff against the merge base. The new internal-audience routing is carried through enqueue, drain, heartbeat filtering, and collect-mode transcript handling with matching tests for the touched behavior."*
- **Observed result after fix:** cron-awareness events queued after isolated cron delivery no longer reach user-facing chat surfaces. The isolated cron continues to deliver its own result on its configured channel (unchanged), and the main session sees the awareness event only as wrapped runtime context for agent reasoning — not as a user-visible bubble. Local `tsgo:core:test` is clean; all 171 touched-surface vitest tests pass; harness PASS end-to-end against `d5fc9d9609`.
- **What was not tested:**
  - No side-by-side pre-patch vs post-patch screenshot of a Matrix client showing the absence of the user-visible `System: cron run …` bubble — the negative-evidence form is hard to capture cleanly because the bubble simply stops appearing. The unit test in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` asserts the `audience: "internal"` is set at the producer site as the structural regression.
  - No `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` capture — Mercury's prod channel is Matrix, not Telegram. Happy to capture Telegram visible proof if a maintainer can point at a usable test account, but cannot self-generate it without channel access.
  - The broader audience-routing primitive (additional producers beyond `queueCronAwarenessSystemEvent`) was intentionally **not** migrated in this PR; that scope is deferred to follow-up case-by-case work per #69492.
  - The rebased squashed commit has not yet been reloaded into Mercury's prod gateway daemon — pending the next `mercury/local-patches` deploy, after which the cron-awareness path will be re-exercised on the new code. The pre-rebase prod evidence above stands in for this gap; the new code path is byte-equivalent in observable behavior (only the internal type-level coupling to the removed sender-owner machinery was dropped).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passes (clean exit; required for test-touching PRs per [`feedback_openclaw_check_gaps.md`](https://example.invalid)-style validation gap closure).
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/infra/system-events.test.ts` passes (53/53).
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/queue.collect.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ghost-reminder.test.ts` passes (118/118).
- [x] `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-pr72201.ts` PASS end-to-end against the rebased HEAD `d5fc9d9609` (see proof section).
- [x] `codex review --base upstream/main` against the rebased HEAD reports no actionable regressions.

## Pre-PR peer review

This proposal was peer-reviewed by `codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -c reasoning_effort=high` before implementation. The peer review caught a contract-confusion issue between two earlier drafts (selective-drain vs wrap-on-drain) and pushed for the wrap-on-drain design landed here. Round-2 verdict: approve with PR-body wording tweak (avoid GitHub auto-close keywords for a partial fix; "Addresses part of #69492" is the framing applied here).

## Degree of testing

Lightly tested. Unit tests cover the primitive default, round-trip, equality, the wrap shape, ordering, edge cases, end-to-end strip via `stripInternalRuntimeContext`, and adversarial delimiter-token escaping. The single migrated producer has a regression assertion in the existing test. No e2e or live-channel coverage added.

target.title: fix(runtime): isolate workers and bound cron top-off
target.body:
## Summary
- wrap Linux service-managed Codex app-server and supervised child launches in user systemd scopes under openclaw-workers.slice, with an opt-out to avoid recursive scopes
- make cron reserve only available run slots, finalize jobs as they finish, and top off freed slots while slower jobs continue
- route stale Codex thread-binding fallback compaction through the Codex OAuth runtime path and add regression coverage around the changed scheduling/isolation paths

## Overlap check
- Open PR #86893 touches src/cron/service/timer.ts, but it only extends the isolated cron cold-runner setup watchdog. This PR changes cron slot reservation/top-off behavior and worker cgroup isolation, so it is related but not duplicate.
- Open PR #86733 touches timer.regression.test.ts only for main-session systemEvent timeout coverage; no implementation overlap with this branch.
- Open PR #86655 touches a Codex transport test while adding a Claude app-server harness; no shared runtime implementation with this branch.
- Open PRs #78164 and #81777 are conceptually adjacent runtime/client-isolation work, but they touch different files and solve different boundaries.

## Validation
- git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.process.config.ts src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.test.ts (19 tests)
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (52 tests)
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/transport-stdio.test.ts (10 tests)
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts (11 tests)
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts (104 tests)

target.title: docs(telegram): note native progress draft control tradeoff
target.body:
## Summary

- Document that Telegram native tool-progress drafts use `sendMessageDraft` and can leave some clients, including Telegram Android, in a passive-viewing state while active.
- Recommend keeping `nativeToolProgress` disabled for workflows that need `/steer`, `/stop`, corrections, or mid-run follow-ups from the same chat.
- Point interactive users at the existing edited preview lane with `toolProgress: true` and `nativeToolProgress: false`.

Fixes #86195

## Tests

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram docs now explain the native `sendMessageDraft` tool-progress tradeoff and the safer interactive-control configuration for users who need to steer or stop active runs from the same chat.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw docs checkout on macOS using the repository docs validation scripts against this patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm docs:list`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm docs:check-mdx`; `pnpm docs:check-links`.

Evidence after fix: After-fix terminal capture from the local docs checkout:

```text
$ pnpm docs:list
$ node scripts/docs-list.js
Listing all markdown files in docs folder:
...
channels/telegram.md - Telegram bot support status, capabilities, and configuration
  Read when: Working on Telegram features or webhooks
...
Reminder: keep docs up to date as behavior changes.

$ git diff --check

$ pnpm docs:check-mdx
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (655 files, 5357ms).

$ pnpm docs:check-links
$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4457
broken_links=0
```

Observed result after fix: The Telegram channel docs render as valid MDX and have no broken internal links after adding the warning about native tool-progress drafts and interactive control.

What was not tested: A live Telegram Android composer-lock reproduction was not run for this docs-only change; the documented behavior is based on the issue report and the existing opt-in OpenClaw `sendMessageDraft` behavior.

## Summary
- add focused coverage for task-domain view mappers
- assert task run and flow DTO field mapping, nested flow details, and summary cloning
- cover implicit flow detail summaries when callers do not provide one

## Test plan
- git diff --check
- PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-domain-views.test.ts

## Duplicate check
- gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "mapTaskFlowDetail" -> []
- gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "task-domain-views" -> only unrelated #84827

Test-only change; no runtime behavior changes.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Task-domain view mappers produce the public task-run and flow-detail DTO shapes, including nested task views and aggregate summaries, without leaking internal registry field names such as `taskId`, `parentFlowId`, or `requesterSessionKey` into the public view.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch (`leno23/test-task-domain-views`) on macOS, using Node with the repository TypeScript loader (`node --import tsx`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
  import { mapTaskFlowDetail, mapTaskRunView } from './src/tasks/task-domain-views.ts';

  const task = {
    taskId: 'task-proof',

target.title: fix(voice-call): avoid duplicate OpenAI initial greetings
target.body:
## Summary

- Avoid firing an explicit `triggerGreeting()` for OpenAI realtime outbound calls when OpenAI server VAD auto-response is active.
- Fold the queued outbound initial greeting into the OpenAI session instructions so the provider-owned first auto-response still receives the caller-facing greeting text.
- Keep the explicit greeting path for providers without OpenAI auto-response and for OpenAI configurations that set `autoRespondToAudio: false`.

Fixes #85846

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm openclaw voicecall --help` (runtime CLI/build smoke; exits because `voice-call` is disabled by default in this checkout)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI realtime outbound calls with a queued initial greeting no longer create two independent greetings when server VAD auto-response is active; the initial greeting is provided through session instructions and `triggerGreeting()` is suppressed for that OpenAI auto-response path.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with the voice-call realtime WebSocket handler exercised through the repository runtime/test harness and an OpenClaw CLI runtime smoke command.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`; `pnpm openclaw voicecall --help`.

Evidence after fix: The realtime handler regression test opens the Twilio-style realtime WebSocket path for an outbound call whose metadata contains `initialMessage: "Hi, this is Milly."`, invokes provider readiness, and verifies `triggerGreeting` is not called while the OpenAI bridge request instructions contain that greeting. A companion regression verifies `autoRespondToAudio: false` still keeps the explicit `triggerGreeting()` path. The targeted Vitest commands and `git diff --check` exited 0. The CLI runtime smoke built OpenClaw and bundled plugin assets, then exited with the expected message that `openclaw voicecall` is provided by the disabled-by-default `voice-call` plugin.

Observed result after fix: OpenAI's auto-response path receives the initial greeting in the session instructions and the voice-call plugin no longer sends a second explicit greeting request for the default OpenAI realtime configuration.

What was not tested: A live Twilio/OpenAI phone call was not placed from this environment; the after-fix runtime proof uses the local realtime WebSocket handler and OpenClaw CLI/build smoke rather than external telephony credentials.

Added

target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** DeepInfra plugin: (1) discovers the full live chat catalog (88 models, vs 7 hardcoded on `main`) — wizard-vs-discovery guard no longer suppresses dynamic results; (2) registers live `image-gen` and `video-gen` catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern); (3) injects Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers on outgoing payloads for `deepinfra/anthropic/*` requests (OpenRouter wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"` before this patch); (4) wires TTS / STT / image-gen / VLM end-to-end through the deepinfra speech, media-understanding, image-gen, and VLM providers. **Real environment tested:** Hosted Lightsail VM (Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0), openclaw `2026.5.21` built from this PR, `openclaw.service` running as a systemd unit. Live network to `api.deepinfra.com`. DeepInfra key configured. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Capability inventory commands (chat catalog + image + audio providers): ```bash pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` Then in the webchat UI: image-gen → VLM → STT (inbound audio attachment auto-transcribed) → TTS in one chat session (full session log attached below). Anthropic cache markers — HTTP-level capture via a small Node logging proxy in front of `api.deepinfra.com`; agent fires one `anthropic/*` turn, proxy records the outgoing body: ```bash openclaw agent --local --model deepinfra/anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 \ --message "Say hello briefly." < /dev/null cat /tmp/cache-proxy.log ``` Test suite: ```bash pnpm test extensions/deepinfra pnpm check:changed ``` **Evidence after fix:** Terminal — `capability` commands show 88 dynamically-discovered chat models, image providers configured, audio providers configured: Onboard summary — default model auto-selected from discovery: Model picker — full discovered DeepInfra catalog (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Google, Meta, MoonshotAI, NVIDIA, Qwen, ZAI, ...): Webchat session JSONL — image-gen → VLM → STT → TTS, all positive outcomes, redacted: [f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172089/f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log) Generated image (1792×1024 JPEG produced by the `image_generate` tool): Passive STT — agent responds to the auto-transcribed inbound audio attachment: STT input: [bobbyanaya-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172146/bobbyanaya-texas-prison-slang-three-greetings-same-meaning-whatx27s-up-165808.mp3) TTS — `tts` tool fires; the chat UI renders a playable audio bubble: TTS output: [voice-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172138/voice-1779514789533---942686ed-8f59-4165-95ce-8e588eafff99.mp3) Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` marker captured on the outgoing HTTPS body to DeepInfra via a logging proxy — proves the deepinfra-local cache wrapper fires where the upstream OpenRouter wrapper would have short-circuited: **Observed result after fix:** Six surfaces work end-to-end on live deployment: dynamic chat catalog (88 models), live image-gen catalog (FLUX), live audio provider catalog, in-chat image generation produces an on-disk JPEG, in-chat passive STT transcribes inbound audio, in-chat TTS produces a playable MP3 attachment, and Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers reach DeepInfra's API on `anthropic/*` requests. `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 69/69 green; `pnpm check:changed` lanes → exit 0. **What was not tested:** Long-form video-gen completion (the request fires successfully; the upstream model just doesn't finish within the `fetchWithSsrFGuard` 60 s window — separate upstream concern); the cache-marker test request itself returned an empty response from Anthropic (unrelated upstream model behavior), but the marker injection is independently captured at the wire by the proxy and shown above. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - Backward compatible at the config layer; no schema changes, no env var renames, no migration script. ### Migration impact Per-change rationale for the few default/fallback adjustments: | Change | Rationale | Upgrade impact | |---|---|---| | Default chat model: `nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B` → `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash` | DeepInfra's current curated recommendation; Nemotron has been retired from the active catalog. | Users on the *implicit* default get DeepSeek-V4-Flash on upgrade. Anyone who pinned a model explicitly via config is unaffected. | | TTS default voice: `af_alloy` → `af_bella` | **Bug fix.** `af_alloy` is an OpenAI voice name and returns HTTP 500 on DeepInfra's Kokoro endpoint (`curl -X POST https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/audio/speech … "voice":"af_alloy"` → `500 Voice ID 'af_alloy' not found`). `af_bella` is Kokoro's actual built-in default and returns valid audio. | TTS was broken for any user on the shipped default. After this PR it works. | | TTS fallback order: Kokoro-82M moved to position [0] (was [1]) | Kokoro accepts the configured default voice without per-model voice mapping; Qwen3-TTS requires a different voice ID. With Qwen first, every out-of-box TTS call failed. | None for users with explicit `messages.tts.providers.deepinfra.modelId` set; the default user gets a working TTS path. | | Image fallback list curated to `[FLUX-1-schnell, FLUX-1-dev, Juggernaut-Lightning-Flux, Qwen-Image-Max]` | Trimmed entries retired from the active DeepInfra catalog; live discovery still picks up newer image models when an API key is configured. | No-key users get a slightly shorter explicit list; keyed users see the full live catalog. | | Video fallback list: unchanged from `main` (`[Pixverse/Pixverse-T2V, Pixverse/Pixverse-T2V-HD, Wan-AI/Wan2.1-T2V-1.3B, google/veo-3.0-fast]`) | Reverted to main's allowlist to avoid surprising existing users; live `registerModelCatalogProvider` picks up newer video models (Wan-AI/Wan2.6-T2V, ByteDance/Seedance-2.0) automatically as the backend tags them. | None. | ### Auth-context threading fix `provider-discovery.ts` now passes the resolved catalog context (`env`, `agentDir`, and `hasApiKey=true` once `buildSingleProviderApiKeyCatalog` has verified the key resolves) through to `discoverDeepInfraModels`. Without this, an auth-profile-only setup could authenticate at the discovery layer yet still receive the static catalog because `hasDeepInfraApiKey` had no `agentDir` to probe the profile store. Both env-var and auth-profile-only setups now receive the live 88-model catalog identically. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed.

Release notes

Sourced from gradle-wrapper's releases.

9.5.1

The Gradle team is excited to announce Gradle 9.5.1.

Here are the highlights of this release:

  • Task provenance in reports and failure messages
  • Type-safe accessors for precompiled Kotlin Settings plugins

Read the Release Notes

We would like to thank the following community members for their contributions to this release of Gradle: atm1020, mataha, Adam, Attila Kelemen, Benedikt Ritter, Björn Kautler, Caro Silva Rode, CHANHAN, Dmitry Nezavitin, Eng Zer Jun, KugelLibelle, Madalin Valceleanu, Markus Gaisbauer, Oliver Kopp, Philip Wedemann, ploober, Roberto Perez Alcolea, Rohit Anand, Suvrat Acharya, Ujwal Suresh Vanjare, Victor Merkulov

Upgrade instructions

Switch your build to use Gradle 9.5.1 by updating your wrapper:

./gradlew wrapper --gradle-version=9.5.1 && ./gradlew wrapper

See the Gradle 9.x upgrade guide to learn about deprecations, breaking changes and other considerations when upgrading.

For Java, Groovy, Kotlin and Android compatibility, see the full compatibility notes.

Reporting problems

If you find a problem with this release, please file a bug on GitHub Issues adhering to our issue guidelines. If you're not sure you're encountering a bug, please use the forum.

We hope you will build happiness with Gradle, and we look forward to your feedback via Twitter or on GitHub.

9.5.0

07/21/2024

Updates `org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-android` from 1.10.2 to 1.11.0
Changelog

Sourced from org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-android's changelog.

Version 1.11.0

Various

  • Kotlin was updated to 2.2.20 (#4545).
  • Improved the published jar files (#3842, #4599).
  • Various documentation improvements, including complete rewrites of structured concurrency and error handling-related KDoc (#4433, #4596).

Breaking changes and deprecations

  • Moved Promise-related functions from JS and Wasm/JS to the new web target. On Wasm/JS, this is a breaking change. Before the change, Promise on Wasm/JS could work with arbitrary Kotlin types, but now, only JsAny subtypes are accepted (#4563).
  • Changed handling of coroutine exceptions that can't be propagated on JS and Wasm/JS. Before, exceptions were logged, but now, they are reported to the JS runtime (#4451, #4631).
  • Deprecated using CoroutineDispatcher as the coroutine context key; now, ContinuationInterceptor has to be used instead (#4333).
  • Advanced the deprecation levels on kotlinx-coroutines-test APIs (#4604).
  • Added lint functions that mark passing a Job to coroutine builders as deprecated (#4435).

Bug fixes and improvements

  • Added a callsInPlace(EXACTLY_ONCE) contract to runBlocking in code shared between JVM and Native (#4368).
  • Added a callsInPlace(EXACTLY_ONCE) contract to suspendCancellableCoroutine (#4574).
  • Fixed flowOn incorrectly handling ThreadContextElement updates (#4403).
  • Fixed exceptions in user-supplied Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler instances causing the internal coroutines machinery to fail (#4516).
  • Fixed CoroutineDispatcher.asScheduler in the RxJava integration not cancelling outstanding work when a Worker gets cancelled, which led to memory leaks in some scenarios (#4615).
  • Fixed SharedFlow entering an invalid state when a subscriber and an emitter are cancelled simultaneously (#4583).
  • Fixed an R8 optimization leading to shareIn/stateIn coroutines getting garbage-collected (#4646). Thanks, @​solevic!

Small additions

  • Added CompletableDeferred.asDeferred for obtaining a read-only Deferred view (#4408).
  • Added SharedFlow.asFlow for obtaining a Flow view with hidden hot flow semantics (#4530). Thanks, @​g000sha256!
  • Added a StateFlow.collectLatest overload returning Nothing to assist with finding unreachable code (#4454).
  • Added ReceiveChannel.consumeTo for consuming a ReceiveChannel into a MutableCollection (#4520).
  • Added a StateFlow<T>.onSubscription overload returning a StateFlow<T>, similar to SharedFlow<T>.onSubscription returning SharedFlow<T> (#4275). Thanks, @​xit0c!
  • Added terminal Flow operators for collecting a Flow to a Map (#1541).

Changelog relative to version 1.11.0

No changes, only the version is increased.

Version 1.11.0-rc02

Restored binary compatibility with 1.10.2 and older versions on Wasm/JS for usages of Promise-related functions (#4661).

Version 1.11.0-rc01

Various

  • Kotlin was updated to 2.2.20 (#4545).
  • Improved the published jar files (#3842, #4599).
  • Various documentation improvements, including complete rewrites of structured concurrency and error handling-related KDoc (#4433, #4596).

... (truncated)

Commits
  • 8564f65 Version 1.11.0
  • a4c6af9 Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/master' into develop
  • ef917b4 KT-84955: mark apple x64 tagets as deprecated error (#4645)
  • 5ebc421 Update the release procedure description (#4670)
  • 95f46a0 Remove old maven repository settings (#4672)
  • b4f4f0a Fix package name of ToMapCollectionSamplesTest. (#4674)
  • 86738dc Added templates to the issue creation wizard (#4654)

  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

target.title: perf(agents): memoize per-message token pressure on precheck path
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` (the per-turn pre-prompt token-pressure precheck on the embedded-runner path) reduces over the entire transcript on every prompt submission via `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` → `estimateMessageTokenPressure`. The per-message work is recomputed every turn even when only a single message has been appended since the last call, so the wall-time cost of the precheck grows linearly with session length on the hot path before the provider request is sent.

Why does this matter now?

Tool-heavy long sessions pay multi-millisecond per-turn CPU on the precheck. Synthetic 1000-message tool-heavy transcripts measure ≈ 2.5 ms / turn pre-cache; with very large `toolCall.arguments` and tool-result text the per-turn cost reaches ≈ 100 ms at 4000 messages. None of this is dominant on its own, but it is purely pre-dispatch local CPU that the same turn pays over and over with no semantic change to the inputs.

What is the intended outcome?

Memoize `estimateMessageTokenPressure` by message object identity using a process-local `WeakMap<object, number>`. Messages on the embedded-runner precheck path are append-only and not mutated in place once added to `activeSession.messages`, so identity is a safe cache key. The reducer in `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` now calls a thin `memoizedMessageTokenPressure` wrapper that returns the cached value on a hit and computes-then-stores on a miss. No semantic change: the numerical output of `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` and the `route` / `overflowTokens` / `estimatedPromptTokens` fields of `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` are byte-identical for any given input.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Wiring `llmBoundaryTokenPressure` from a real-rendered-transport-bytes anchor (the canonical-fact-forward direction introduced in #85541). This PR caches the fallback path; it does not compete with that direction.
- `estimateMessagesTokens` in `compaction.ts` (compaction-event path) and the existing prune loop in `compaction.ts`.
- `estimateToolResultReductionPotential` in `tool-result-truncation.ts`, which is the un-cached residual cost still present in the precheck wall time.
- Anthropic-side payload / cache-control work.
- Media path work.

What does success look like?

The committed replay artifact (`scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`) demonstrates the post-patch cold-to-warm wall-time ratio on a 1000-message tool-heavy synthetic transcript (≈ 4× on the run captured in Evidence below). Earlier local profiling against an uncached build of the same code showed per-turn savings in the 1.9–2.0× range on representative tool-heavy long sessions and 5–7× on a pathological extreme synthetic shape; this PR does not claim TTFT, p95/p99, or any provider-side improvement. No semantic change, all existing tests pass, six new behavior tests pin the cache contract.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The cache key contract: identity is a safe key only because the embedded-runner path treats messages as append-only. The new test "memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate" documents that cached estimates are tied to message identity, so callers must preserve the append-only / no-in-place-mutation invariant or add explicit invalidation.
- WeakMap lifecycle: the cache is bounded by live message objects and cannot leak.
- Boundary semantics around assistant tool-call arguments, tool-result content, and image blocks are unchanged.

## Identity-cache invariant and reproducible benchmark

Reproducible benchmark artifact: `scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`. It directly calls the production `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` helper used by the embedded-runner at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:4125`, with the same parameter shape and an `activeSession.messages`-shaped transcript. It does not run the full provider/request loop. It captures cold/warm/append-one timings plus route stability across 10 append turns.

Caller inventory for `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` (these are audited existing callers, not additional files changed by this PR):

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:4125` — embedded-runner prompt-submission loop. Messages source: `activeSession.messages` (pi-coding-agent state). Append-only; replacement via `state.messages = limited` creates new identities.
- `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts:515` — `openclaw compact` CLI. Messages source: `getSessionBranchMessages(sessionManager)` (read from session JSONL). Each CLI invocation loads from disk → new objects, no cross-call identity reuse.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-result-context-guard.ts:419` — mid-turn tool-result truncation guard. Messages source: `contextMessages` from `transformContext`. When truncation is needed, `cloneMessagesForGuard` (`tool-result-context-guard.ts:132–135`) creates new top-level identities, then `enforceToolResultLimitInPlace` (`185–199`) mutates only those clones. When no truncation is needed, `contextMessages === sourceMessages` and no in-place mutation runs.

External callers via plugin-sdk: `src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.ts:219` re-exports `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` and the rendered-boundary helper for plugin authors. The plugin-sdk re-export is not runtime-frozen; external plugin consumers could mutate message objects in place. Safety there is by documented input contract: callers that repeat estimates must treat message objects as immutable for the cache lifetime, or pass fresh message identities after mutation. The in-tree exported callers audited above do not mutate cached identities before repeated estimates. The contract is named in the doc-comment block above the `messageTokenPressureCache` declaration in `preemptive-compaction.ts` and pinned by the per-message identity cache test #2.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

This PR does not close an issue. It addresses a structural cost identified in local profiling that overlaps in shape with reports such as #76702 (Windows + Feishu DM slow large-session latency) and #86231 (Codex harness prompt latency overhead). It deliberately does not claim to resolve either, because neither has been root-caused to the precheck path; the per-turn precheck saving alone is too small to explain those reports.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #85541 (the PR that reshaped this estimator into `estimateLlmBoundaryTokenPressure` and introduced `llmBoundaryTokenPressure` as a caller-supplied anchor).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: per-turn `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` CPU wall time on the embedded-runner precheck path scales O(N) with transcript size and recomputes the same per-message token pressure on every turn for unchanged messages.
- Real environment tested: Linux 6.8.0-111-generic, Node 22.22.2, branch `feature/openclaw-preemptive-precheck-cache` on `8da8bc4aadfc7f62af864b24718896b538c069e3`. Production function `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` is the call under measurement, via the same import path the embedded-runner uses.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --version` → v22.22.2
  - `PROBE_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) pnpm exec tsx scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts`
- Evidence after fix:

Live console output from running the committed replay artifact against the production embedded-runner precheck function — a copied terminal capture, not a unit test summary:

```
$ PROBE_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) pnpm exec tsx scripts/perf/preemptive-precheck-replay.ts
openclaw preemptive-precheck-replay (embedded-runner path)
commit                      03a0e995d50713da6f458c265a29fa5198ecdb7e
messages_in                 1010
budget_tokens               200000
reserve                     16000

cold_precheck_ms            7.460
warm_precheck_ms_mean       1.759 (20 repeats over the same transcript)
append_step_ms_mean         1.408 (10 turns, one new user msg each)

per-append measurements:
  turn  1 ms=   1.341 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1555832
  turn  2 ms=   1.344 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1555911
  turn  3 ms=   1.331 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1555990
  turn  4 ms=   1.598 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556069
  turn  5 ms=   1.395 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556148
  turn  6 ms=   1.334 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556228
  turn  7 ms=   1.893 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556307
  turn  8 ms=   1.309 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556386
  turn  9 ms=   1.262 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556465
  turn 10 ms=   1.269 route=compact_then_truncate  estimatedPromptTokens=1556544

final_route                 compact_then_truncate
final_estimatedPromptTokens 1556544
final_overflowTokens        1372544
final_promptBudgetBefRes    184000
```

- Observed result after fix: cold first scan pays the full per-message reducer once; subsequent scans against the same activeSession.messages-shaped transcript avoid recomputing the cached per-message estimate and drop to roughly a quarter of the cold time on this hardware. The route, `estimatedPromptTokens`, `overflowTokens`, and `promptBudgetBeforeReserve` returned by the production function remain semantically consistent across the 20 warm scans and grow monotonically across the 10 append-one turns — confirming the cache is returning correct values for each fresh message identity, not stale estimates.
- What was not tested: no live provider/channel ingress (no Anthropic/OpenAI call); no captured real session JSONL replay (the transcript is synthetic but uses the production message shape and the production exported helper); no Windows or non-x86_64 hardware run; no claim of TTFT or provider-side improvement.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: synthetic transcript, single machine, single Node version. Absolute numbers are hardware-dependent; the cold-to-warm ratio (≈ 4× here) is the part that generalizes. The replay harness can be re-run by any reviewer on a normal worktree (no provider credentials required).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

Listed under "Exact steps or command run after this patch" above.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Six new behavior tests in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts`, all inside a new `describe("per-message identity cache", …)` block:

1. Same boundary estimate on repeat scans of the same messages.
2. Memoizes per message identity so post-call content mutation does not alter the estimate (documents the identity-key contract).
3. Re-counts when a brand-new message identity is appended.
4. Does not double-count when the same message identity appears twice.
5. Preserves precheck `route` / `overflowTokens` / `estimatedPromptTokens` decisions across repeat calls.
6. Counts assistant tool-call argument bytes identically on repeat scans (covers the heavy-`JSON.stringify` path).

What failed before this fix, if known?

No prior test failures. The change is a performance optimization, not a correctness fix.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` returns byte-identical decisions for any given input. The only observable difference is reduced wall time on the precheck path in long tool-heavy sessions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The identity-cache invariant for callers outside `activeSession.messages`. The plugin-sdk re-export at `agent-harness-runtime.ts:219` means external plugin authors can call `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` themselves; if any such caller mutates a message object in place, the cached estimate is stale.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Caller inventory: every in-tree caller of `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` was inspected (see "Real behavior proof and identity-cache invariant" above). `attempt.ts` uses append-only `activeSession.messages`; `cli-compaction.ts` reads from disk each call (fresh objects); `tool-result-context-guard.ts` only mutates clones produced by `cloneMessagesForGuard`. None of the in-tree callers mutate cached message objects.
- Documented contract: the doc-comment block above `messageTokenPressureCache` names the invariant and explicitly cites the test that pins it.
- Behavior test: `preemptive-compaction.test.ts` per-message identity cache test #2 mutates a message's content after the first scan and asserts that the second scan returns the unchanged estimate, so any caller that introduces in-place mutation will see its expected fresh value silently shadowed by the cached value — the test will continue to document the contract.
- Repo policy alignment: process-local metadata caches are explicitly permitted by the repo `AGENTS.md` ("Process-local metadata caches ok when lifecycle-owned and bounded/single-slot. Freshness exceptions need named owner + tests"); this cache is process-local, lifecycle-bounded by the WeakMap-to-message reference, single-slot per message, and covered by the named test contract.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper review 2026-05-25T22:11Z requested two rank-up moves: (a) a reproducible benchmark artifact that exercises `shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt` on the embedded-runner path, and (b) caller-inventory confirmation that exported callers outside `activeSession.messages` cannot mutate cached message objects before a repeated estimate. Both are addressed above. Pre-submission self-review also softened benchmark wording ("synthetic … long-session-shaped" rather than "representative"), clarified the cached-estimate phrasing to match the measured table, removed a non-diff line reference (`compaction.ts:535`), and reframed the cache-mutation test as documenting the identity-key invariant rather than asserting a regression-failure.

target.title: fix(ui): keep terminal webchat run status after stale session rows
target.body:
## Summary
- Reconcile locally terminal chat run state against stale current-session rows from `sessions.list` and `sessions.changed`.
- Prevent stale `status: "running"` rows from re-enabling the webchat abort/In progress UI after the terminal status toast expires.
- Add focused coverage for the stale `sessions.list` and `sessions.changed` races described in #86939.

Fixes #86939.

## Credit
Reported and root-caused by @scotthuang in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86939.

## Implementation Notes
- Add a small helper in `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts` that detects a locally terminal current chat run via `chatRunStatus` when `chatRunId` is already null.
- Apply that helper to current-session rows from both `loadSessionsOnce` and `applySessionsChangedEvent` before they can make `hasAbortableSessionRun` true again.
- Keep the rewrite narrow: only rows for the current session that are still active/running should be normalized to `hasActiveRun: false` with `status: "done"` for done runs or a non-running interrupted status for interrupted runs.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Webchat no longer gets stuck showing `In progress` after a completed model call when a stale session row arrives after the terminal chat event.
Real environment tested: To be completed by the fix executor; this planning worker verified the current source path on main SHA 5ef812293b08be065badac552c5656aaaf34093d.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Run the focused Vitest command above, then `pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: Focused tests should prove stale `sessions.list` and `sessions.changed` rows are normalized before the webchat abortable state is derived.
Observed result after fix: The composer keeps the terminal Done/Interrupted state and does not fall back to a stale In progress label after the toast clears.
What was not tested: Live browser/gateway reproduction unless the executor adds manual Control UI proof.

ClawSweeper 🐠 replacement reef notes:
- Cluster: issue-openclaw-openclaw-86939
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Issue #86939 was reported and root-caused by @scotthuang; preserve that credit in the PR body and release-note context.; No source PR exists for this cluster, so source_prs is intentionally empty.
- Validation: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts; pnpm check:changed
- Replacement reason: ClawSweeper could not update the source PR branch directly, so it opened a writable replacement PR instead.
