target.title: fix(ui): ignore stale running rows after terminal chat
target.body:
## Summary
- keep a terminal current chat session from being revived by stale `sessions.list` or `sessions.changed` rows with `status: "running"`
- preserve real newly started runs by allowing newer `startedAt` rows after the previous terminal `endedAt`
- add regressions for stale list rows, stale websocket rows, and late stale websocket rows after the run-status toast clears
- include a lint-only cleanup for redundant `image-tool.ts` provider-registry casts required by current `pnpm check:changed`

Fixes #86939.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Webchat can flip back from `Done` to permanent `In progress` when a stale current-session row with `status: "running"` arrives after the terminal chat event has already cleared the local `chatRunId`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `27ff166db9d6`, Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, real controller/runtime modules executed via `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a current dashboard session row already terminal (`status: "done"`, `hasActiveRun: false`, `chatRunId: null`), applied a stale `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "running"` and `hasActiveRun: true`, then checked `hasAbortableSessionRun()`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the runtime command:

```text
environment=node v22.22.0 platform=linux
beforeAbortable=false
{
  "applied": true,
  "change": "updated"
}
{
  "key": "agent:main:dashboard",
  "kind": "direct",
  "updatedAt": 3,
  "hasActiveRun": false,
  "status": "done",
  "startedAt": 1,
  "endedAt": 2,
  "sessionId": "sess-main"
}
afterStaleEventAbortable=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The stale running update was applied for metadata freshness but normalized back to `hasActiveRun: false` and `status: "done"`, so abortable chat UI stays disabled after the terminal run-status toast clears.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser dashboard was driven manually; the proof uses the real session controller and chat abortability predicate, with UI/controller regression coverage below.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts --pool forks --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=tap`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Note: running all three recommended UI test files in one `run-vitest` invocation timed out after 240s in this local environment with no failure output, but the three files passed when run individually.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated/managed chat images are served through authenticated thumbnail previews and full-size Open/Download/Copy targets. Current head: `9f63b88296432c0d775eff026b0bea3eb40a500b`; behavior proof below was captured at `1f608477f2d9bfaadcac24bef246d766061b7192`, and later heads only change the CI Node fallback script.
- Real environment tested: Local PR checkout on Linux, focused Gateway test shard, Vite-served Control UI module, system Chromium `/usr/bin/chromium` through Playwright with `--no-sandbox`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the managed-image Gateway test shard, then served a temporary Control UI proof page with Vite, rendered `renderMessageGroup` from `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts`, waited for the managed image blob preview, hovered the image frame, clicked Open/Download/Copy, and captured the browser-evaluated fetch/action state.
- Evidence after fix:

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there is no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- Why it matters: Plugin UIs currently require bespoke Control UI wiring, which makes real plugin UI surfaces harder to build, test, and keep out of core.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. A separate proof plugin lives on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only the plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree. It demonstrates a real app-nav Control UI plugin that searches, inspects, injects, and resumes prior sessions through the entry-point API added by this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: A plugin can register a Control UI app-nav entry, have it listed by the gateway, launch it through a short-lived token, and render it in Control UI without hardcoded plugin UI code. Current head tested: `87e108f70eb4eb2b7b5c540b9a574bebe0845c84`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout in the PR worktree after merging latest upstream main, plus the running OpenClaw development gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789` with the real Session Search plugin registered as a Control UI app-nav entry. Browser proof used Playwright against system Chromium and the live Control UI; gateway token was used locally and redacted from output.

The branch is also merged with latest upstream main, the missing UI spawn helper exports are restored while keeping Corepack fallback behavior, the host-hook fixture registers the new entry-point seam, and generated i18n metadata is clean.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts`

  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`

- Scenario the test should lock in: Plugin UI entry-point registration, gateway listing, launch-token validation, plugin HTTP auth behavior, and Control UI navigation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the plugin API, gateway auth/protocol seam, and Control UI navigation without depending on one specific plugin implementation.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugins can now add authenticated Control UI navigation entries through the plugin API. No default UI changes are visible unless a plugin registers an entry point.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[plugin registers route] -> [no generic Control UI nav entry]

After:
[plugin registers entry point]
  -> [gateway lists entry point]
  -> [Control UI renders navigation item]
  -> [launch token opens authenticated plugin UI route]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No

- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Plugins can expose Control UI entry points, so entry points are scoped and served through gateway-authenticated plugin routes.
  - Launch uses short-lived plugin UI entry tokens instead of unauthenticated direct access.
  - Entry points declare required operator scopes.
  - Tests cover unauthorized access rejection and launch-token validation.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux

### Actual

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`

target.title: fix(ui): ignore stale running rows after terminal chat
target.body:
## Summary
- keep a terminal current chat session from being revived by stale `sessions.list` or `sessions.changed` rows with `status: "running"`
- preserve real newly started runs by allowing newer `startedAt` rows after the previous terminal `endedAt`
- add regressions for stale list rows, stale websocket rows, and late stale websocket rows after the run-status toast clears
- include a lint-only cleanup for redundant `image-tool.ts` provider-registry casts required by current `pnpm check:changed`

Fixes #86939.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Webchat can flip back from `Done` to permanent `In progress` when a stale current-session row with `status: "running"` arrives after the terminal chat event has already cleared the local `chatRunId`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `27ff166db9d6`, Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, real controller/runtime modules executed via `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a current dashboard session row already terminal (`status: "done"`, `hasActiveRun: false`, `chatRunId: null`), applied a stale `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "running"` and `hasActiveRun: true`, then checked `hasAbortableSessionRun()`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the runtime command:

```text
environment=node v22.22.0 platform=linux
beforeAbortable=false
{
  "applied": true,
  "change": "updated"
}
{
  "key": "agent:main:dashboard",
  "kind": "direct",
  "updatedAt": 3,
  "hasActiveRun": false,
  "status": "done",
  "startedAt": 1,
  "endedAt": 2,
  "sessionId": "sess-main"
}
afterStaleEventAbortable=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The stale running update was applied for metadata freshness but normalized back to `hasActiveRun: false` and `status: "done"`, so abortable chat UI stays disabled after the terminal run-status toast clears.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser dashboard was driven manually; the proof uses the real session controller and chat abortability predicate, with UI/controller regression coverage below.

## Verification

target.title: feat(memory-core): Memory Privacy Audit + Encrypted Backup
target.body:
## Summary
Build the first contribution as **privacy visibility plus encrypted backup**, not live encrypted memory. The feature adds a safe CLI workflow that audits where OpenClaw stores memory-related data, flags privacy risks, and supports encrypted export/import of memory artifacts.

### Key Changes
- Add CLI commands:
  - `openclaw memory privacy audit`
  - `openclaw memory export --encrypted --out <file>.age`
  - `openclaw memory import --encrypted --in <file>.age --target <dir>`
- Audit reports:
  - memory workspace path
  - `MEMORY.md`, `memory/*.md`, `DREAMS.md`, `memory/.dreams/`, and dreaming reports
  - SQLite/vector index path and size
  - embedding provider classification: local, remote, none, or unknown
  - Active Memory transcript persistence state
  - plaintext memory surfaces and backup risks
- Encrypted backup:
  - export creates a tar archive of selected memory artifacts, then encrypts it
  - output uses the `.age` format via the TypeScript `age-encryption` package
  - import decrypts into an explicit target directory
  - import refuses to overwrite existing files by default
  - no live memory files are changed during export

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Adds memory privacy visibility plus encrypted backup/restore at rest. This proof covers `openclaw memory privacy audit`, non-empty `openclaw memory export --encrypted`, and `openclaw memory import --encrypted`, after the symlink hardening and CI fixes in commit `b7749a4f36`.

**Real environment tested:** Isolated local lab only: `/Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/openclaw`, with `OPENCLAW_HOME` pointed at `/Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home`. The real production OpenClaw home `/Users/anandsagar/.openclaw` was not used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```console
$ export OPENCLAW_HOME=/Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home
$ export OPENCLAW_MEMORY_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE='proof-passphrase'
$ node openclaw.mjs memory privacy audit
$ node openclaw.mjs memory export --encrypted --out /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/memory-backup.age
$ node openclaw.mjs memory import --encrypted --in /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/memory-backup.age --target /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore
$ shasum -a 256 /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/MEMORY.md /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home/.openclaw/workspace/DREAMS.md /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/DREAMS.md /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home/.openclaw/workspace/memory/2026-05-12.md /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/memory/2026-05-12.md
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the isolated lab run:
```console
commit=b7749a4f36
PROOF_ROOT=/Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512
--- audit ---
Memory Privacy (main)
Workspace: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace
Embeddings: auto (unknown)
Session transcripts: not found (0 files)
Artifacts:
  memory-file · present · 1 files · 13 B
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md
  memory-directory · present · 1 files · 11 B
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/memory
  dreaming-artifact · present · 1 files · 12 B
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/DREAMS.md
  dreaming-artifact · missing
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/memory/.dreams
  index · present · 1 files · 4.0 KiB
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite
  sessions · missing
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions
Findings:
  warning unknown-embedding-provider Memory embedding provider "auto" could not be classified as local or remote.
  warning plaintext-memory-artifacts Memory, dreaming, or session artifacts are stored as readable local files.
  warning memory-index-present A memory index exists on disk and may reveal keywords or semantic metadata.
  action encrypted-backup-available Use `openclaw memory export --encrypted` to create an encrypted backup before moving or archiving memory.

--- export ---
Encrypted memory backup written: /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/memory-backup.age (3 files)
--- import ---
Encrypted memory backup restored: /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore (3 files)
--- restored sha256 ---
8a210d26f9c51fc3715e1e68d8ee104ed695736b8ec86b1303ec1c9fdd1d48c7  /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md
8a210d26f9c51fc3715e1e68d8ee104ed695736b8ec86b1303ec1c9fdd1d48c7  /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/MEMORY.md
2ed0cf4d155b05160c72a330b70d82cad2d0db29e993096c84b6c7d0a9925d5b  /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home/.openclaw/workspace/DREAMS.md
2ed0cf4d155b05160c72a330b70d82cad2d0db29e993096c84b6c7d0a9925d5b  /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/DREAMS.md
8ad2de45d66039fd07774f7b5fba346d2cbb4ac84d55a2b9aeef3690073e5bd9  /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/home/.openclaw/workspace/memory/2026-05-12.md
8ad2de45d66039fd07774f7b5fba346d2cbb4ac84d55a2b9aeef3690073e5bd9  /Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/memory/2026-05-12.md
--- restored files ---
/Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/DREAMS.md

/Volumes/Untitled/openclaw-privacy-lab/proof-20260512-190512/restore/memory/2026-05-12.md
```

**Observed result after fix:** The audit detected the non-empty lab memory artifacts and warned about plaintext memory/index surfaces. The encrypted export wrote a `.age` backup containing 3 files. The encrypted import restored 3 files, and SHA256 hashes matched between source and restored `MEMORY.md`, `DREAMS.md`, and `memory/2026-05-12.md`.

**What was not tested:** Live encrypted memory is intentionally out of scope for this phase. I did not test production `/Users/anandsagar/.openclaw`; all proof used the isolated lab home above.

target.title: fix(telegram): treat ENETDOWN as transient network failure
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A transient `ENETDOWN` from Telegram/SSRF outbound connect is now classified with the existing transient/pre-connect network failures instead of falling through to fatal gateway handling.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime modules on macOS from `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-86688`, using the real Telegram network classifier, gateway unhandled-rejection classifier, and cron retry classifier with a redacted Bot API `connect ENETDOWN` error shape.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... EOF` runtime smoke that imports `extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.ts`, `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.ts`, and `src/cron/retry-hint.ts`, tags a redacted Telegram `sendMessage` ENETDOWN failure, and verifies all production classifiers return the expected recovery decisions.

Evidence after fix:

```text
OpenClaw ENETDOWN runtime smoke (redacted):
telegramRecoverable=true
telegramSafeSendRetry=true
gatewayTransientNetwork=true
gatewayBenignUncaught=true
cronNetworkRetry=true
```

I also ran the focused regression suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts src/infra/unhandled-rejections.fatal-detection.test.ts src/cron/retry-hint.test.ts`, which passed 5 test files and 165 tests. New assertions cover `ENETDOWN` as a Telegram recoverable error, a safe pre-connect send retry, a Telegram fetch fallback retry trigger, a benign uncaught network exception, a non-fatal uncaught exception case, and a cron `retryOn: ["network"]` match.

Observed result after fix: The same redacted Telegram-style `TypeError: fetch failed` with nested `code: ENETDOWN` is now accepted as recoverable for Telegram, safe to retry for pre-connect sends, transient/benign for gateway process-level handling, and retryable for cron network retry hints. This follows the sibling local network/connect failures (`ENETUNREACH`, `EHOSTUNREACH`, etc.) without changing SSRF hostname/IP allow/block policy.

What was not tested: I did not use a live Telegram bot token or physically flap a macOS network interface during live polling; the live terminal evidence above is a local OpenClaw runtime smoke over the real production classifier modules, supplemented by focused regression tests.

## Summary

- Add `ENETDOWN` to Telegram recoverable and pre-connect network error classification.
- Let Telegram transport fallback retry on `ENETDOWN` connect failures.
- Treat `ENETDOWN` as a transient/benign uncaught network failure in the gateway safety net.
- Include `ENETDOWN` in cron network retry hints for consistency with sibling network codes.

Fixes #86688.

## Verification

- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... EOF` redacted OpenClaw ENETDOWN runtime smoke
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts src/infra/unhandled-rejections.fatal-detection.test.ts src/cron/retry-hint.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 4 - Constrained/device-bound provider capabilities - Role: Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. - Head: `0295ff8709` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `0295ff8709` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds Phase 4 constrained/device-bound provider metadata to the Channel Broker contract. - Covers WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles-backed iMessage bridges as broker provider capabilities, not native plugin rewrites. - Stacks on Phase 3 official/app capability matrix (#86164), Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86114 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86148, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds `constraints` and `badges` to broker platform/provider capabilities. - Models constrained provider facts such as: - `businessApi`, `cloudApi`, `providerHosted` - `deviceBound`, `linkedDevice`, `qrPairing`, `sessionFragile` - `selfHosted`, `phoneNumberRequired`, `signalCli` - `macHostRequired`, `messagesSignedIn`, `privateApiOptional`, `privateApiRequired` - `externalBridge` - Exposes resolved provider capabilities in channel-broker status snapshots so UI/status can show constrained-provider badges. - Adds WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage target proof through `channel-broker`. - Documents constrained provider expectations in Channel Broker, SDK, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles migration docs. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Constrained providers expose hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed limits through broker `constraints`/`badges`, and WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage still route through provider-owned broker requests. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that evaluated constrained-provider capability support and called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` for `broker:whatsapp:direct:15551234567`, `broker:signal:direct:+15551234567`, and `broker:imessage:group:chat-guid-1?threadId=imessage-thread-1`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "constrainedProviders": { "whatsappBusiness": true, "whatsappDeviceBound": false, "signalDeviceBound": true }, "outbound": [ { "label": "whatsapp-direct", "platform": "whatsapp", "conversation": { "id": "15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "signal-direct", "platform": "signal", "conversation": { "id": "+15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "imessage-group", "platform": "imessage", "conversation": { "id": "chat-guid-1", "type": "group", "threadId": "imessage-thread-1" } } ] } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path distinguished WhatsApp Business-style support from device-bound WhatsApp requirements, accepted Signal's self-hosted/device-bound constraint set, and produced broker provider requests for WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage. - **What was not tested:** Live WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, or BlueBubbles-backed provider traffic; this PR proves the broker contract/schema/docs layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/status.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not implement Phase 5 legacy-plugin freeze. - Next phase: #86115 freezes new legacy native-channel feature work after broker migration paths exist. This PR supplies the constrained-provider metadata needed before that freeze can be credible.

target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and trusted inbound peer candidates for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, follow-up routed replies, durable turn replies, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit-send compatibility by keeping explicit `message.send`, gateway sends, and message-tool sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Persists `sendPolicyMode` for queued delivery replay and treats legacy queued entries without that field as explicit replay, preserving pre-PR queue semantics.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

Latest head: `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`

Focused recovery, deliver, and send-policy validation requested by ClawSweeper:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       110 passed (110)
```

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.20s
Tests 14 passed
```

Additional focused validation for the explicit-send footprint trim and durable reply callers:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 30.40s
Gateway shard: 51 passed
Infra shard: 52 passed
Agents shard: 59 passed
Channels shard: 55 passed
```

Type/lint checks:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue-recovery.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/skill-tool-dispatch.runtime.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/plugins/tool-types.ts
```

Result:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core passed
oxlint passed
```

## Footprint reduction

The PR surface was reduced from ClawSweeper's reviewed `43 files / +1185 -58` to:

```text
30 files changed, 1038 insertions(+), 58 deletions(-)
```

Removed from the PR footprint:

- explicit message-tool `inboundPeer` propagation
- plugin tool-context `inboundPeer` propagation
- message-action-runner explicit-send `inboundPeer` propagation
- outbound-send-service explicit-send `inboundPeer` propagation
- related tests whose only purpose was proving explicit paths carried `inboundPeer`

Reasoning: explicit sends intentionally bypass `session.sendPolicy`, so passing `inboundPeer` through those explicit paths was unnecessary surface area. The automatic/durable reply path remains covered.

## Legacy queue compatibility

Recovery now treats queued delivery entries without `sendPolicyMode` as explicit replay:

```text
sendPolicyMode: entry.sendPolicyMode ?? "explicit"
```

This preserves pre-existing queued `message.send` / gateway retry behavior after upgrade under `session.sendPolicy.default: "deny"`.

Regression added:

```text
src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts
- replays legacy queued entries without sendPolicyMode as explicit under deny-by-default policy
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` must allow same-peer automatic Telegram durable replies and suppress wrong-peer automatic durable replies before platform send. The same PR also preserves explicit-send compatibility for legacy queued entries without `sendPolicyMode`.

- **Real environment tested:** Live Telegram bot setup using OpenClaw PR-head source at proof time, with a real Telegram direct peer `TG_PEER_A` and a distinct redacted target `TG_PEER_B`. Latest live Telegram proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676. Current code-review head is `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`; the live proof head was `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** On current head `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`, I reran the focused recovery, deliver, send-policy, durable-reply, gateway-send, message-tool, typecheck, and lint commands listed in the Verification section. The live Telegram proof flow used the same durable `sendDurableMessageBatch` policy path: configure `session.sendPolicy` to deny direct Telegram when `{ "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }`, send one automatic durable reply to `TG_PEER_A`, then attempt an automatic durable send to `TG_PEER_B` in the same inbound-peer context.

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live Telegram proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676. Copied redacted runtime output from that proof:

```text
Inbound Telegram direct session: agent:main:telegram:direct:TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC
Inbound peer candidates: TG_PEER_A, TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC, TG_PEER_A_USERNAME

Same-peer allow:
  outbound target: TG_PEER_A
  durable result: sent
  Telegram message id: 6
  Telegram API calls for this case: 1

Wrong-peer suppression:
  attempted outbound target: TG_PEER_B
  durable result: suppressed
  suppression reason: denied_by_send_policy
  cancel reason: send_policy_peer_mismatch
  Telegram API calls for this case: 0
```

Redacted diagnostic excerpt:

```json
{
  "status": "suppressed",
  "reason": "denied_by_send_policy",
  "hookEffect": {
    "cancelReason": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
    "metadata": {
      "code": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
      "peerEquals": "inboundPeer",
      "expectedPeers": ["TG_PEER_A", "TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC", "TG_PEER_A_USERNAME"],
      "actualPeer": "TG_PEER_B"
    }
  },
  "telegramApiCallsForCase": 0
}
```

Required proof gate status on current PR body/head:

```text
$ gh pr checks 85763 --repo openclaw/openclaw
Real behavior proof  pass  15s  https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26369003419/job/77617646289

$ gh run view 26369003419 --repo openclaw/openclaw --log
External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` delivered with one Telegram API send call. Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed with `denied_by_send_policy` / `send_policy_peer_mismatch` and zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message. Current head additionally preserves legacy queued explicit sends by replaying missing `sendPolicyMode` as `explicit`, covered by `src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`.

- **What was not tested:** The real Telegram send itself was captured on proof head `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`, not repeated after the final `86f2586a` recovery/footprint commit. That final commit is limited to legacy queue replay compatibility and explicit-send footprint cleanup, while the current PR body and head now pass the required `Real behavior proof` gate at `26369003419`.

### Config under test

`session.sendPolicy` denied direct Telegram sends when the outbound peer did not equal the trusted inbound peer candidates:

```json
{
  "default": "allow",
  "rules": [
    {
      "action": "deny",
      "match": {
        "allOf": [
          { "channel": "telegram" },
          { "chatType": "direct" },
          { "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Proof verdict

- Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` was delivered.
- Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed by `session.sendPolicy`.
- Suppression included `denied_by_send_policy` and `send_policy_peer_mismatch`.
- The wrong-peer case recorded zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message.
- Token, real chat id, phone number, username, and message content beyond the proof marker are redacted.

target.title: fix(config): trust mergeScopedSearchConfig-injected legacy web-search keys in validator
target.body:
## Summary

- Teach `ToolsWebSearchSchema` in `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` to skip the legacy-shape rejection (`legacy web_search provider config must use plugins.entries.<plugin>.config.webSearch`) for keys that an installed plugin's `mergeScopedSearchConfig` has just injected at gateway boot — the exact path responsible for the fresh-deploy crash documented in #86779.
- Have `mergeScopedSearchConfig` (`src/agents/tools/web-search-provider-config.ts`) call a new `markLegacyWebSearchPluginKeyTrusted(key)` exported by the schema module whenever it writes a key under `tools.web.search[<id>]`. The merge function is the natural source of truth for "this legacy-named key was injected by an installed plugin shim, not hand-written"; the validator then trusts only those keys.
- Hand-written legacy `tools.web.search.<provider> = { … }` blocks that no installed plugin claims still fail validation with the same `use plugins.entries.<plugin>.config.webSearch` pointer — the strict rejection that's documented in repo guidance is preserved end-to-end.
- Add 7 focused vitest cases in `src/config/zod-schema.web-search-plugin-shim.test.ts` covering: hand-written legacy rejection, post-merge acceptance, no over-trust spillover to other legacy keys, `mergeScopedSearchConfig` end-to-end before/after, `undefined` plugin config = no trust mark, non-record values pass through untouched, and `clearTrustedLegacyWebSearchPluginKeysForTest` reverts strict rejection.

## Behavior change to flag

Cold gateway startup on `2026.5.22` no longer rejects configs whose `tools.web.search.<plugin>` shape was written by the plugin's compatibility shim. Operators on BLUE/GREEN deploys that hit `Gateway failed to start: Invalid config at /data/.openclaw/openclaw.json. tools.web.search.perplexity: legacy web_search provider config must use plugins.entries.<plugin>.config.webSearch` are unblocked without touching `openclaw.json`. Hand-written legacy configs still fail validation; the message is unchanged. The two existing call sites of `mergeScopedSearchConfig` (re-exported through the `provider-web-search*-contract.ts` Plugin SDK surfaces) gain an idempotent `Set.add(key)` side effect that's bounded to the process lifetime and clearable from tests.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `ToolsWebSearchSchema` now consults a process-local `trustedLegacyWebSearchPluginKeys` set in its `superRefine`. `mergeScopedSearchConfig` populates that set with the same key it just wrote into `searchConfig[key]`. The crash chain from #86779 (mergeScopedSearchConfig writes `tools.web.search.perplexity` → validator rejects → gateway refuses to start) is broken at the validator step.
- **Real environment tested:** local macOS arm64 source checkout (post-rebase against `upstream/main`); behavior verified through the new `src/config/zod-schema.web-search-plugin-shim.test.ts` suite that drives `ToolsSchema.safeParse({ web: { search: { … } } })` directly with and without the trust mark, and end-to-end via `mergeScopedSearchConfig(...)` → `ToolsSchema.safeParse(...)` against the published `ToolsSchema` export. Existing `src/agents/tools/web-search.test.ts` (18 cases) and `src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts` (full suite) still pass — the merge function's added `Set.add` side effect is structurally safe.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `pnpm test src/config/zod-schema.web-search-plugin-shim.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/tools/web-search.test.ts src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.post-compaction-guard.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test:changed`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `ToolsSchema.safeParse({ web: { search: { provider: "perplexity", perplexity: { apiKey: "k" } } } })` returns `success: false` with the legacy issue on `web.search.perplexity` before any merge runs — the strict rejection for hand-written configs survives.
  - The same `ToolsSchema.safeParse(...)` returns `success: true` after `mergeScopedSearchConfig({ provider: "perplexity" }, "perplexity", { apiKey: "k" })` is called once, mirroring what happens at gateway boot when the perplexity plugin's manifest carries `compatibilityRuntimePaths: ["tools.web.search.apiKey"]`.
  - Marking `perplexity` as trusted does **not** unblock the `brave` legacy shape — the trust set is per-key and has no spillover.
  - `mergeScopedSearchConfig(..., undefined)` is a no-op for the trust set (the function early-returns before any mutation), so the validator still rejects the same key on subsequent parses.
- **Observed result after fix:**
  - `src/config/zod-schema.web-search-plugin-shim.test.ts`: 7 / 7 passed.
  - `src/agents/tools/web-search.test.ts` + `src/config/config.web-search-provider.test.ts` + `src/config/schema.test.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.post-compaction-guard.test.ts`: 18 + 65 + N + N = 101 / 101 passed across 4 files (no regression from the existing legacy-rejection or scoped-merge tests).
  - `pnpm check:changed`: exit 0 (typecheck core + extensions + tests, lint, import-cycles 0 runtime value cycles, plugin-sdk wildcard / dependency-pin / package-patch guards all green).
  - `pnpm test:changed`: 1869 / 1871 passed across 28 shards. The 2 failures in `src/commands/onboard.test.ts` (`fails fast for invalid --secret-input-mode`, `fails fast for invalid --reset-scope`) reproduce on pristine `upstream/main` with this patch reverted and have no overlap with `src/config/**` or `src/agents/tools/web-search-provider-config.ts`.
- **What was not tested:** the original fresh-container reproduction from #86779 (`openclaw@2026.5.22 (a374c3a)` inside `node:22-bookworm-slim` Docker, perplexity/gemini/kimi plugins installed, gateway boot succeeding instead of crashing with the legacy-shape error). The fix is verified at the validator + merge integration that owns the rejection; the container-side production timeline and the "Exact-bytes copy of working BLUE config to GREEN" reproduction in #86779 come from the reporter's setup.

## Notes

- The trust set lives at module scope in `zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` and is cleared between tests via `clearTrustedLegacyWebSearchPluginKeysForTest()` — gateway plugin metadata is process-stable (root `AGENTS.md`: "Process-local metadata caches ok when lifecycle-owned and bounded/single-slot"), so a single mutable Set is the right scope here. The set is keyed by the plugin's `tools.web.search.<key>` segment, not the plugin id, so each `mergeScopedSearchConfig(searchConfig, key, …)` call registers exactly the key it wrote.
- The merge function is the only call site needed for registration today: the two Plugin SDK contract barrels (`provider-web-search-config-contract.ts`, `provider-web-search-contract.ts`, `provider-web-search.ts`) re-export `mergeScopedSearchConfig` without rewrapping it, so registration happens automatically wherever a plugin's compatibility shim runs.
- Option B from the issue ("validator skips the legacy-shape check for keys that match an installed plugin's `compatibilityRuntimePaths`") is implemented through this auto-registration handshake rather than a separate manifest-discovery pass, which keeps the schema module pure (no transitive `src/plugins/**` import) and the diff small. Options A and C from the issue (rewriting the merge target shape, doctor-side migration) remain available as follow-up cleanups but are not needed to unblock the fresh-deploy crash.
- Reviewed against root `AGENTS.md`, `src/agents/tools/CLAUDE.md`, `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md`. No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING).

Refs #86779

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:

- What you did **not** verify:

  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: a046242234bbbdc369730a38fc9b0b2d1be9d211.

target.title: fix(doctor): warn on stale prompt cache config
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw doctor` warnings for cache-ttl configs whose selected chat model/provider cannot keep the prompt-cache window warm.
- Covers direct Anthropic, Anthropic-compatible providers, Bedrock Claude, Google prompt-cache models, OpenAI-family unsupported routes, aliases, auth-profile suffixes, subagent/channel routes, inherited agent defaults, fallback models, and heartbeat model/cadence mismatches.
- Keeps the advisory lightweight: no new config seam and no provider-runtime plugin loading from doctor.
- Includes a separate small lint cleanup in `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`; the changed gate was failing on stale casts from current main.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested by maintainer during beta cache-hit investigation after `/status` reported 0% cache hits with cache-ttl expectations.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor` now warns when cache-ttl pruning is configured with a model/provider setup that will not maintain the expected prompt-cache window.
- Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on final head `5a0b2df21419cffc4f8976eb7ed860025412ba9b` over `origin/main` `d1c8f09b006e0b1e01ca60ac725aa80a2e702a31`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox/Testbox `tbx_01ksjcqg4m7xs3q2gffp9z7553`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26456278519, exit 0.
- Observed result after fix: changed gate completed with typecheck core, typecheck core tests, lint core, runtime import cycle guard, and changed-surface guard checks passing.
- What was not tested: live provider cache-hit behavior against external model APIs.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local `pnpm test:serial` in this Codex worktree hit the known no-TTY pnpm install trap; direct `tsx` smoke probes passed, the focused prompt-cache Vitest file passed during autoreview, and the full changed gate passed remotely.
- Before evidence: beta `/status` showed `Cache: 0% hit · 0 cached, 20k new` in a cache-ttl setup.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- direct `node --import tsx` smoke probes for direct Anthropic clean config, custom `anthropic-messages` explicit-retention warnings, Bedrock Claude explicit-retention warnings, DeepInfra Anthropic static eligibility, normalized provider API lookup, OpenRouter heartbeat mismatch warnings, strict-agent fallback inheritance, and Moonshot/Zai eligibility alignment
- `codex review --base origin/main`: no actionable correctness issues; targeted `src/commands/doctor/shared/prompt-cache-config-warnings.test.ts` passed, 34 tests
- Crabbox/Testbox `corepack pnpm check:changed`: `tbx_01ksjcqg4m7xs3q2gffp9z7553`, exit 0

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added focused doctor warning coverage for retention windows, default TTL/heartbeat behavior, OpenAI-family unsupported routes, aliases, auth-profile suffixes, legacy `cacheControlTtl`, normalized configured provider APIs, default/fallback model resolution, per-agent overrides, subagent/channel routes, heartbeat mismatch behavior, plugin/static provider eligibility, explicit-retention requirements for Google/custom Anthropic-compatible/Bedrock Claude routes, strict-agent fallback behavior, and Moonshot unsupported cache-ttl behavior.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Prior changed-gate attempts caught TS import/fixture issues in the new tests and a current-main oxlint stale-cast failure; all were fixed before this PR was opened.
- Autoreview found normalized provider API lookup, provider-runtime import weight, and Moonshot runtime/advisory alignment issues; all were fixed before this update.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor can now emit advisory warnings for non-ideal prompt cache configs.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config writes or migrations change. This is advisory-only.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. Doctor uses static/runtime-aligned checks and avoids loading provider runtime plugins for this advisory.

What is the highest-risk area?

False positives or false negatives on proxy/provider cache semantics.

How is that risk mitigated?

The advisory mirrors runtime cache-retention rules where possible, avoids broad plugin loading, uses explicit retention requirements only where runtime needs explicit cache config, and covers aliases/channel/subagent/inherited routes in tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI on the draft PR.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings for normalized configured provider APIs, runtime import weight, strict-agent fallback behavior, and Moonshot runtime/advisory alignment were addressed. Final autoreview reported no actionable correctness issues.

target.title: fix(session-store): rewrite generated transcript paths on rollover
target.body:
## Summary

- Rewrites generated `sessionFile` paths when a session-store merge changes `sessionId`.
- Prevents stale generated transcript paths like `old-session.jsonl` or `old-session-topic-456.jsonl` from being carried onto a new session id.
- Preserves custom/non-generated `sessionFile` paths when OpenClaw cannot safely derive a replacement.

Refs #65564.

Complements #73809 by hardening generic session-store merges. The heartbeat-specific archival bug is handled by #73809; this PR keeps other session-store writers from persisting a generated transcript path that still embeds the previous session id after a merge rolls the session id forward.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generic session-store rollover can preserve a stale generated `sessionFile` while changing `sessionId`, causing a new logical session id to keep appending to an old transcript file. This is a session-store invariant hardening change, not the heartbeat-specific reset archival fix from #73809.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node v22.20.0, writing a real temporary `agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` file through the agent session-store persistence path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-session-rollover-proof.mjs
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from running the command above:

```text
OpenClaw session rollover proof
source path: /tmp/openclaw-upstream
store path: /tmp/openclaw-rollover-proof-dsfpZV/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
real path exercised: persistSessionEntry -> updateSessionStore -> mergeSessionEntry
before.sessionId: 11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111
before.sessionFile.basename: 11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111-topic-456.jsonl
rollover patch carried stale sessionFile basename: 11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111-topic-456.jsonl
after.sessionId: 22222222-2222-4222-8222-222222222222
after.sessionFile.basename: 22222222-2222-4222-8222-222222222222-topic-456.jsonl
expected generated sessionFile basename: 22222222-2222-4222-8222-222222222222-topic-456.jsonl
persisted object matches store: true
stale sessionFile carried after fix: false
rewritten to current sessionId: true
result: PASS
```

Observed result after fix: The on-disk `sessions.json` entry kept the new `sessionId` and rewrote the generated transcript basename from `11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111-topic-456.jsonl` to `22222222-2222-4222-8222-222222222222-topic-456.jsonl`; the stale generated `sessionFile` was not carried forward. This proves the generic merge boundary now preserves the invariant that generated transcript paths follow the current persisted session id.

What was not tested: Full repository test suite, live heartbeat runtime, and packaged npm install/upgrade path.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): bound pendingFinalDelivery heartbeat replay by attempt cap and TTL
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `PENDING_FINAL_DELIVERY_MAX_HEARTBEAT_ATTEMPTS = 10` and `PENDING_FINAL_DELIVERY_TTL_MS = 24h`, plus a pure `classifyPendingFinalDeliveryBound` helper that returns `"retry-limit" | "expiry" | null` so the reason vocabulary stays aligned with the existing `suspendPendingFinalDelivery({ reason: "retry-limit" | "expiry" })` in `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts`.
- Wire the bound into the heartbeat-replay else branch of `getReply` (`src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts`) so an orphan `pendingFinalDelivery` (process killed mid-deliver, channel disabled, bot token rotated, etc.) cannot re-emit the cached text forever — the case in #85743 with `pendingFinalDeliveryAttemptCount: 189` and a 4-day-old `pendingFinalDeliveryCreatedAt` now expires on the next heartbeat tick.
- Extract a local async `clearPendingFinalDeliveryEntry()` inside the existing `if (sessionEntry?.pendingFinalDelivery && …)` block so the existing `heartbeatPending.shouldClear` path and the new bound-triggered clear share the same persist semantics; both leave `sessionStore[sessionKey]` and the on-disk store entry with every `pendingFinalDelivery*` field cleared.
- When the bound trips, emit a `logVerbose` carrying reason / attempt count / age-in-seconds / session key, run the clear, and fall through (no `return { text: replayText }`) — the rest of `getReply` then proceeds as if no pending delivery existed. This matches the fail-safe intent in the issue: the legitimate replay-after-restart path still works (fresh `createdAt`, low attempt count), only stuck orphan buffers are short-circuited.

## Behavior change to flag

Sessions with `pendingFinalDelivery: true` that survive 10 successful heartbeat replays or sit on the same `pendingFinalDeliveryCreatedAt` for over 24 hours are now cleared on the next heartbeat (a `[verbose] pendingFinalDelivery suspended …` log line is emitted) instead of having `pendingFinalDeliveryText` re-emitted indefinitely. Users hit by the orphan-buffer pattern in #85743 stop receiving the stale "CRITICAL ALERT" replay; everyone else is unaffected because successful delivery and `heartbeatPending.shouldClear` still own the common-case clearing path, and the bound only triggers strictly after the limits.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `getReply` heartbeat-replay branch now consults `classifyPendingFinalDeliveryBound` before incrementing `pendingFinalDeliveryAttemptCount`. The exact production case described in #85743 — `attemptCount: 189`, `createdAt` ≈ 4 days ago — now returns `"retry-limit"`, which routes through the shared clear and skips the replay return.
- **Real environment tested:** local macOS arm64 source checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` (post-rebase against `upstream/main`); behavior verified through the new colocated unit test surface `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.heartbeat-replay-bounds.test.ts` (10 cases exercising production-style counts, boundary equality, TTL strict-greater-than, undefined / non-finite `createdAt`, retry-limit-vs-expiry tie-break, and `maxAttempts`/`ttlMs` overrides). The wired-in branch in `get-reply.ts` is hand-checked against the existing `heartbeatPending.shouldClear` arm — the new path uses the same extracted clear helper, so the persist semantics on `sessionStore[sessionKey]` and the on-disk store entry are structurally identical.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.heartbeat-replay-bounds.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test:changed`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - All 10 bounds-helper cases pass, including the explicit `(attemptCount: 189, createdAt: now − 4d) → "retry-limit"` case mirroring the reporter's `sessions.json` snapshot.
  - The boundary cases pin `attemptCount === max → "retry-limit"`, `nowMs − createdAt === ttl → null` (strict `>`), `attemptCount > max ∧ createdAt > ttl → "retry-limit"` (retry-limit wins), and `Number.NaN` / `Infinity` createdAt → treated as unknown age, no expiry.
- **Observed result after fix:**
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.heartbeat-replay-bounds.test.ts`: 10 / 10 passed (2.7s).
  - `pnpm check:changed`: exit 0 (typecheck core + extensions + tests, lint core/extensions/scripts, import-cycles 0 runtime value cycles, all repo-wide guards green).
  - `pnpm test:changed`: 255 / 257 passed across 16 shards. The 2 failures in `src/commands/onboard.test.ts` (`fails fast for invalid --secret-input-mode`, `fails fast for invalid --reset-scope`) reproduce on pristine `upstream/main` with this patch reverted (verified by running `pnpm test src/commands/onboard.test.ts` after `git stash` of the patch — 2 / 7 fail with `expected "vi.fn()" to be called once, but got 2 times` against `runtime.error`) and have no overlap with `src/auto-reply/**`. They are pre-existing main failures, not regressions from this change.
- **What was not tested:** a long-lived production runtime actually exhibiting the 189-attempt heartbeat loop and observing the loop collapse after the patch. The fix is verified through the pure-helper coverage plus the structural match to the existing clear arm; the production timeline, `sessions.json` counters, and observed behavior come from the reporter's setup on `2026.5.18`, not mine.

## Notes

- The new helper and constants are `export`ed because they are unit-tested next to the function they serve, and because the issue explicitly suggested keeping the thresholds local so they can graduate to config later — the `maxAttempts` / `ttlMs` overrides on the helper give us that path without leaking config keys today.
- The extracted `clearPendingFinalDeliveryEntry` is a local arrow inside the existing `if (sessionEntry?.pendingFinalDelivery && …)` block, not a module-level helper. It closes over `sessionEntry`, `sessionStore`, `sessionKey`, and `storePath` so the persist semantics match the in-place clear that already existed; no new public surface is exposed.
- This is the fail-safe described in #85743; the common-case clear-after-success and `target === "none"` paths from #83184 / #83187 / #84679 still own the routine clearing flow.
- Reviewed against root `AGENTS.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md`. No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING).

Refs #85743

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there is no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- Why it matters: Plugin UIs currently require bespoke Control UI wiring, which makes real plugin UI surfaces harder to build, test, and keep out of core.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. A separate proof plugin lives on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only the plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree. It demonstrates a real app-nav Control UI plugin that searches, inspects, injects, and resumes prior sessions through the entry-point API added by this PR.
