- Observed result after fix: The Control UI app navigation displayed the real Session Search plugin entry, clicking it launched a sandboxed iframe through `plugins.uiEntryPointLaunch`, the iframe rendered the plugin title/description inside Control UI, and unauthenticated direct route access stayed blocked with `401 Unauthorized`. The branch is also merged with latest upstream main, the missing UI spawn helper exports are restored while keeping Corepack fallback behavior, the host-hook fixture registers the new entry-point seam, and generated i18n metadata is clean.
- What was not tested: I did not rerun the full hosted GitHub matrix locally; GitHub CI is responsible for the complete shard set. The live browser proof used an existing development gateway and plugin instance rather than starting a separate throwaway gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`
  - `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Plugin UI entry-point registration, gateway listing, launch-token validation, plugin HTTP auth behavior, and Control UI navigation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the plugin API, gateway auth/protocol seam, and Control UI navigation without depending on one specific plugin implementation.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugins can now add authenticated Control UI navigation entries through the plugin API. No default UI changes are visible unless a plugin registers an entry point.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[plugin registers route] -> [no generic Control UI nav entry]

After:
[plugin registers entry point]
  -> [gateway lists entry point]
  -> [Control UI renders navigation item]
  -> [launch token opens authenticated plugin UI route]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No

- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Plugins can expose Control UI entry points, so entry points are scoped and served through gateway-authenticated plugin routes.
  - Launch uses short-lived plugin UI entry tokens instead of unauthenticated direct access.
  - Entry points declare required operator scopes.
  - Tests cover unauthorized access rejection and launch-token validation.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Local source checkout, Node.js, pnpm/corepack
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI + gateway plugin host

### Steps

2. Register a plugin with `registerControlUiEntryPoint`.
3. Start the gateway from the rebuilt checkout.
4. Open Control UI.
5. Confirm the plugin entry appears in app navigation.
6. Open the plugin entry.
7. Attempt direct unauthenticated access to the plugin route.

### Expected

- Registered plugin UI entry appears in Control UI navigation.
- Opening the entry launches the plugin UI through authenticated gateway plumbing.
- Direct unauthenticated route access is rejected.

### Actual

- Entry rendered in Control UI.
- Plugin UI opened through the Control UI entry.
- Direct unauthenticated plugin route returned `401`.

target.title: fix: repair Discord approval runtime resolution
target.body:
## Summary
- Read the operator approval runtime token from the shared exec approvals socket config when available, keeping the process-local random token as a fallback.
- Give the operator approval runtime client both `operator.approvals` and `operator.write` so native approval route notices can send through Discord.
- Show an ephemeral Discord follow-up when an approval button is clicked after the request is already resolved or expired instead of making the button look dead.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord approval buttons and native approval route notices use the shared approval runtime credential path. The approval runtime client can now both resolve approvals and send origin/fallback notices, and stale button clicks produce a visible ephemeral response instead of silently doing nothing.
- Real environment tested: Spartacus/OrbStack Docker gateway running the rebuilt OpenClaw image for this PR branch with bundled Discord extension enabled. Gateway health was `running healthy`; `openclaw config validate` passed in the container.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt the local Spartacus image from this PR branch, recreated the live gateway container, then ran a Node script inside `openclaw-orbstack-bot-openclaw-gateway-1` using the built `withOperatorApprovalsGatewayClient` runtime path. The script created a fresh two-phase exec approval routed from Discord, listed it through the approval-runtime client, fetched the allowed decisions, and resolved it through the same approval-runtime path that Discord approval buttons use.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
REQUESTED {"status":"accepted","id":"codex-live-approval-runtime-...","createdAtMs":...,"expiresAtMs":...}
LIST_HAS_ID true
GET_ALLOWED ["allow-once","allow-always","deny"]
RESOLVED {"ok":true}
```

The live gateway logs for the same validation window also showed Discord send succeeding through the native approval route notice path:

```text
[ws] ⇄ res ✓ send 427ms channel=discord ...
```

Before the `operator.write` scope fix, this same route-notice path failed with `missing scope: operator.write`.
- Observed result after fix: A fresh approval was accepted, visible to the approval-runtime client, reported the expected allowed decisions, and resolved with `{"ok":true}` through the approval-runtime client path used by Discord buttons. The Discord route notice send also succeeded instead of failing for missing `operator.write`. Focused regression coverage verifies that an already-resolved or expired Discord approval click sends the ephemeral follow-up `That approval request is no longer pending. It may have expired or already been resolved.`
- What was not tested: I did not attach a screenshot/video of a human clicking the Discord UI button. The live container proof exercises the same gateway approval-runtime client path used by the Discord button handler, and unit coverage verifies the Discord interaction follow-up behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The copied live output is redacted to avoid exposing approval IDs and private Discord details. The proof intentionally uses terminal output and redacted runtime logs rather than screenshots.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this scope fix, the live Discord route-notice path logged `missing scope: operator.write`; before the stale-click UX fix, the Discord button handler swallowed `not-found` results with no follow-up.

## Tests and validation

- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts`
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage updated:
- `src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts` asserts the approval runtime client requests `operator.write` with `operator.approvals`.
- `extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts` asserts stale/already-resolved approval clicks produce an ephemeral follow-up.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: the approval-runtime credential and Discord native approval delivery path.

Mitigation: keep the token source scoped to the existing exec-approvals socket token/fallback behavior, add focused regression coverage, and prove the live approval-runtime request/list/get/resolve path works in the running Discord gateway.

## Current review state

Next action: wait for CI and maintainer/security review on the approval-runtime credential boundary.

Waiting on: CI on the latest pushed head, plus maintainer acceptance of using the shared exec-approvals socket token as the approval-runtime credential.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: real behavior proof was updated with copied live output from the running Spartacus Discord gateway.

- Scenario the test should lock in: Plugin UI entry-point registration, gateway listing, launch-token validation, plugin HTTP auth behavior, and Control UI navigation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the plugin API, gateway auth/protocol seam, and Control UI navigation without depending on one specific plugin implementation.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugins can now add authenticated Control UI navigation entries through the plugin API. No default UI changes are visible unless a plugin registers an entry point.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[plugin registers route] -> [no generic Control UI nav entry]

After:
[plugin registers entry point]
  -> [gateway lists entry point]
  -> [Control UI renders navigation item]
  -> [launch token opens authenticated plugin UI route]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - Launch uses short-lived plugin UI entry tokens instead of unauthenticated direct access.
  - Entry points declare required operator scopes.
  - Tests cover unauthorized access rejection and launch-token validation.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Local source checkout, Node.js, pnpm/corepack

6. Open the plugin entry.

### Expected

- Registered plugin UI entry appears in Control UI navigation.
- Opening the entry launches the plugin UI through authenticated gateway plumbing.
- Direct unauthenticated route access is rejected.

### Actual

- Plugin UI opened through the Control UI entry.

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: open, download, and copy
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably
- Binds paired Control UI read-token media fetches to the requested session key before serving managed image bytes
- Renders generated image frames with the same rounded clipping as other chat media
- Pre-scales known generated-image dimensions so the frame matches the visible thumbnail/image size
- Reserves pending preview space while managed image blobs load, preventing chat scroll/window jumps
- Serves timeline images from configurable thumbnail routes while keeping open/download/copy on the full image
- Adds `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` for local/managed Control UI thumbnails
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract

## Why

Generated images were being displayed without the full media affordances we expect in chat. The implementation also needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry so the new actions attach to the visible image, rounded corners clip the actual image, and the chat viewport does not jump while a managed preview is still loading.

The follow-up fixes make the preview/full-image split explicit: right-click View Image uses the thumbnail preview, while click/Open/Download/Copy use the original full-size image.

## Testing

- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm config:schema:check`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 900s pnpm test test/vitest/vitest.acp.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.shared-core.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.tasks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.utils.config.ts`
- Browser proof: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI chat image component in system Chromium via Playwright, then clicked Open, Download, and Copy.

## Branch Notes

This branch is based directly on latest `upstream/main` and intentionally does not include PR #75727 gateway changes.

## Real behavior proof

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: a046242234bbbdc369730a38fc9b0b2d1be9d211.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts\n\n RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517\n\n Test Files  1 passed (1)\n      Tests  78 passed (78)\n   Duration  12.41s\n\n[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.72s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:

```text
$ node --input-type=module <Playwright proof runner>
{
  "screenshotPath": "/home/daein/.openclaw/workspace/logs/pr-watch/75727-ui-proof-2ce0267.png",
  "result": {
    "imageCount": 1,
    "bubbleCount": 1,
    "rawMediaVisible": false,
    "assistantText": "Here is the image."
  }
}
```

Proof setup: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI renderMessageGroup implementation from this PR branch in system Chromium via Playwright. The message shape matched the normalized gateway result after the raw MEDIA transcript turn is replaced: one assistant text block plus one input_image content block, with no MEDIA directive left in visible text.

- What was not tested: I did not repeat a native Windows visual smoke; the browser proof covers the Control UI rendering path and the terminal proof covers the transcript/media rewrite behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No

- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.

### Steps

2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.

target.title: fix(anthropic): pass system prompt on every turn for claude-cli backend
target.body:
## Problem

Regression introduced in `f7b71abf48` (v2026.4.25, _"fix(agents): pass Claude system prompt via file"_).

When `--system-prompt` was replaced by `--append-system-prompt-file`, `systemPromptWhen` was hardcoded to `"first"` on the Anthropic claude-cli backend. This silently drops the `systemPromptOverride` on every resumed or compacted session — the model receives only the default Claude Code system prompt with zero operator context.

Empirically confirmed: API payloads captured from a live bot show the operator system prompt present on turn 1 (fresh session, `isNew=true`) and completely absent on turn 2+ (resumed session, `useResume=true`).

Tracked in #80374. This PR completes what #81199 started (which only fixes path 1).

## Root cause

Four code paths all gate on `useResume === false` / `isNewSession === true`, assuming the system prompt is only needed once:

| # | File | Gate |
|---|------|------|
| 1 | `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts` | `systemPromptWhen: "first"` (hardcoded default) |
| 2 | `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts` | `!useResume && systemPromptArg` (file write skipped on resume) |
| 3 | `src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.ts` | `!params.useResume` (arg not pushed on resume) |
| 4 | `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` | `stripLiveProcessArgs(..., useResume)` strips prompt flags on resume (affects stream-json / live-stdio bots) |

## Fix

- **Path 1**: change default to `systemPromptWhen: "always"` for the bundled Anthropic backend
- **Paths 2 & 3**: keep existing `!useResume` logic but also allow through when `systemPromptWhen === "always"`
- **Path 4**: only strip system-prompt flags when `systemPromptWhen !== "always"`

No behaviour change for backends that explicitly set `systemPromptWhen: "first"` or `"never"`.

## Testing

`pnpm tsc --noEmit` passes. The change is a 7-line diff across 4 files.

target.title: fix(qqbot): persist group inbound delivery route
target.body:
## Summary

- Persist QQ Bot group/guild inbound delivery metadata through the channel turn record contract so later session delivery can use the exact inbound route.
- Preserve the exact QQ group target from the inbound event for announce/session delivery instead of relying only on session-key fallback parsing.
- Keep direct C2C/DM messages from overwriting the main session delivery route.
- Add regression coverage around group route persistence and announce delivery behavior.
- Out of scope: adding or changing live QQ Bot credentials, production bot configuration, or unrelated channel routing behavior.

Reviewers should focus on QQ Bot route persistence, sessions_send announce target resolution, and whether the fallback path still behaves correctly for non-QQ channels.

## Linked context

Closes #86387

Related current review finding: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86723#issuecomment-4539178842

No maintainer-specific request beyond the linked issue and current ClawSweeper feedback is known.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QQ Bot group/guild inbound route persistence and later outbound reply or sessions_send announce delivery back to the same group target, including the stored exact group route and accountId.
- Real environment tested: Source checkout validation on Linux worker for commit 871ca99c1b on fix/qqbot-group-route-persistence. A live QQ Bot group runtime was not available in this worker environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts; CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled; CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types; git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD; git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused validation on 871ca99c1b reported Test Files 2 passed (2), Tests 49 passed (49) for the agents shard, Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 6 passed (6) for the QQ Bot extension shard, and [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 12.54s. Bundled extension lint exited 0. Test typecheck ran tsgo:core:test and tsgo:extensions:test and exited 0. git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD exited 0. git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main exited 0 with tree 4222e937fdb1db310e56f8c0bf363a28a300ead9.
- Observed result after fix: QQ Bot announce target resolution now opts into stored session lookup, and the sessions_send regression sends the announce payload to qqbot:group:CAAC3E9D0D1C21018767EF4E6ED45CCA with accountId qq-main instead of the key-derived group:caac3e9d0d1c21018767ef4e6ed45cca fallback.
- What was not tested: Live QQ Bot group message processing against the external QQ service.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This worker has no QQ Bot credential variables or live QQ service target, so it cannot safely produce the redacted live group proof requested by ClawSweeper.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper reported that sessions_send announce resolution still derived QQ Bot delivery from the session key before reading stored session deliveryContext.

## Tests and validation

Completed on 871ca99c1b:

- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts — passed (49 agent tests and 6 QQ Bot extension tests).
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled — passed.
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types — passed.
- git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD — passed.
- git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main — passed (4222e937fdb1db310e56f8c0bf363a28a300ead9).

Regression coverage added/updated: QQ Bot outbound dispatch route persistence, announce-target lookup from stored QQ Bot deliveryContext, and sessions_send announce delivery using the persisted exact QQ Bot group route/account.

Known current gap: live QQ Bot runtime proof is still unavailable from this worker.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: routing outbound QQ Bot replies or sessions_send announcements to the wrong target.

Risk mitigation: keep route persistence limited to group/guild inbound targets, preserve direct-message guard behavior, and cover announce delivery with focused regression tests.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper re-review requested for 871ca99c1b after fixing the P1 announce-route finding.

Current status: code fix and focused validation are complete on the current head. The PR still documents the live QQ Bot proof limitation because this worker does not have external QQ Bot credentials or a safe live group target.

target.title: fix(skills): sync plugin skills to sandbox workspace
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #86190 Plugin skills (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/`) are stored as symlinks pointing to real directories in installed packages. In sandbox mode, `syncSkillsToWorkspace` should copy skills into the sandbox workspace so the `read` tool can access them at `/workspace/skills/...`. However, `loadGeneratedPluginSkillRecords` did not populate `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` for plugin skills. As a result, sandboxed agents saw host-only symlink paths in `` that fail with: ``` Path escapes sandbox root: ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md ``` ## Root Cause `loadContainedSkillRecords` was called without setting `syncSourceDir` for plugin skills, so: - `skill.filePath` pointed to symlink path (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/...`) - `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` were undefined - `syncSkillsToWorkspace` couldn't copy the skill to sandbox ## Fix Create `setSyncSourceForPluginSkill` helper that sets only `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` without modifying `baseDir`/`filePath`. This: 1. Preserves symlink path as `baseDir` for display purposes 2. Sets `syncSourceDir` to the real directory path for syncing 3. Enables `syncSkillsToWorkspace` to copy from real path 4. Skills appear as real directories in `/workspace/skills/` ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin skills under `~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/` (symlinks) were not synced to the sandbox workspace by `syncSkillsToWorkspace`. Sandboxed agents could not load plugin-provided skills because the `read` tool rejected host-only symlink paths with "Path escapes sandbox root". - **Real environment tested**: Windows 11 + WSL2 + Docker Desktop, Node.js v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.22 built from source (commit 53bd8a5), Sandbox image: `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`, Sandbox mode: `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode=all`, Model: `tencent-coding-plan/glm-5` - **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: 1. Built sandbox Docker image: `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh` 2. Started gateway with sandbox mode: `node openclaw.mjs gateway` 3. Started TUI client: `node openclaw.mjs tui --local` 4. Sent request to trigger skill loading: "Use the browser-automation skill to explain its purpose" 5. Verified skills synced inside sandbox container: `docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 bash -c 'ls -la /workspace/skills/'` 6. Verified SKILL.md readable inside sandbox: `docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 bash -c 'head -30 /workspace/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md'` - **Evidence after fix**: **Screenshot 1 - Sandbox container running (Docker Desktop):** Docker Desktop showing `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim` container running. **Screenshot 2 - Plugin skills synced to /workspace/skills/:** Shows `browser-automation` (plugin skill from symlink) successfully synced as real directory alongside other skills like `weather`, `github`, `notion`, etc. **Screenshot 3 - SKILL.md readable from sandbox:** Shows the skill content successfully read from `/workspace/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md` inside the sandbox container - no "Path escapes sandbox root" error. - **Observed result after the fix**: - Sandbox container started successfully with `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim` image - 60+ plugin skills synced to `/workspace/skills/` as real directories (not symlinks) - SKILL.md files readable from inside sandbox container - Agent successfully loaded and explained the `browser-automation` skill - No "Path escapes sandbox root" errors in gateway logs - **What was not tested**: N/A - full end-to-end agent request completed successfully. ## Unit Test Evidence ``` npx vitest run src/agents/skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts ====================================================================== Issue #86190: Plugin skills unreadable in sandbox ====================================================================== [Step 1] Setup - Create real plugin skill directory Path: .../real-plugin-skill-2 Content: SKILL.md with name=wiki-maintainer [Step 2] Create symlink under ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/ Symlink: .../plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer Target: .../real-plugin-skill-2 (This is how OpenClaw exposes plugin skills) [Step 3] Create target sandbox workspace Path: .../target-1 (This becomes /workspace in the sandbox container) [Step 4] Run syncSkillsToWorkspace - Key fix: setSyncSourceForPluginSkill enables sync Sync completed successfully [Step 5] Verification Target skills/: wiki-maintainer wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md exists: true Directory type: real directory (FIX WORKS!) ====================================================================== TEST PASSED - Fix verified ====================================================================== Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 6 passed (6) ``` ## Test Plan - [x] New test: `skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts` with step-by-step verification - [x] Gateway startup with sandbox mode - no path escape errors - [x] Sandbox container running with skills synced - [x] SKILL.md readable from inside sandbox container - [x] All existing sync tests pass - [x] All existing plugin-skills tests pass

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
